{{神社 |名称 = 小社神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Ogoso-jinja (Naiku) 01.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[玉城町]]小社曽根字小社山1955 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 46.8 | 経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 38 | 経度秒 = 52.8 |祭神 = 高水上命 |社格 = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}}末社 |創建 = [[延暦]]23年（[[804年]]）以前 |本殿 = [[神明造]] |別名 = 雨の宮さん |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Ogosō Shrine''' (小社神社) is a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|摂末社}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} (Inner Shrine) within the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} complex. This shrine enshrines a {{ill|suijin|ja|水神|lt=water deity}}, and it is located in an area that was historically the base of the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏}}, who served as {{ill|negi (Shinto)|ja|禰宜}} (senior priests) at the Inner Shrine and developed the surrounding region.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing, ed. (2008), p.58.</ref>
[[Category:Branch shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[ja:小社神社]]
== Overview ==
Ogoso Shrine (小社神社) is located in Ogoso Sone, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture. It is one of the 13 auxiliary and subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine (Naiku) of Ise Grand Shrine. Among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine, it ranks 4th. The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 7,014.9 square meters. Locally, it is affectionately known as "Ame no Miya" or "Ame no Miya-san" and has been a place for rain-making prayers during droughts.
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is Takaminakami no Mikoto (高水上命), a water deity also worshipped at {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社}} within Tamaki Town. The sacred object of worship is a stone.
== History ==
Ogoso Shrine was established by Yamatohime-no-Mikoto. According to the definition of subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, it was founded before the completion of the "Enryaku Gishikicho" in 804. The Arakida clan, who developed the area as the sacred rice fields of the Inner Shrine, were based in Ogoso Sone and revered Ogoso Shrine as their clan deity. The "Kenkyu Third Year Annual Events of the Imperial Grand Shrine" (1192) records that the clan visited the shrine on the first Monkey day of the 4th lunar month.
During the Sengoku period, the Arakida clan relocated to Uji, where the Inner Shrine is located, due to the incursion of warrior factions, leading to the decline of Ogoso Shrine. In the early Meiji period, the Grand Shrine of Ise aimed to revive 21 auxiliary and subordinate shrines whose shrines had ceased to exist. In 1874, the restoration of {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社}}, {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)}} was accomplished. In 1875, a petition was submitted to the Ministry of Religion for the restoration of five more shrines, but it was not granted. However, in 1880, Ogoso Shrine and {{ill|Ashihara Shrine|ja|葭原神社}} were reconstructed using old materials from the East and West storehouses of {{ill|Mishioden Shrine|ja|御塩殿神社}}. The remaining 15 shrines were never restored.
The shrine was reconstructed in March 1911.
== Access ==
Ogoso Shrine is surrounded by rice fields and is located slightly off Mie Prefectural Route 717 Iwadetamura Line.
=== Public Transport === * Approximately a 25-minute walk southeast (about 2.0 km) from Tamura Station on the JR Sangu Line.
=== By Car === * Approximately 10 minutes (about 5.0 km) from Tamaki IC on the Ise Expressway. There is no parking available nearby.

The enshrined deity is Takaminakami no Mikoto (高水上命), a water deity also worshipped at {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社}} within Tamaki Town. The sacred object of worship is a stone.

Ogoso Shrine was established by Yamatohime-no-Mikoto. According to the definition of subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, it was founded before the completion of the "Enryaku Gishikicho" in 804. The Arakida clan, who developed the area as the sacred rice fields of the Inner Shrine, were based in Ogoso Sone and revered Ogoso Shrine as their clan deity. The "Kenkyu Third Year Annual Events of the Imperial Grand Shrine" (1192) records that the clan visited the shrine on the first Monkey day of the 4th lunar month.
During the Sengoku period, the Arakida clan relocated to Uji, where the Inner Shrine is located, due to the incursion of warrior factions, leading to the decline of Ogoso Shrine. In the early Meiji period, the Grand Shrine of Ise aimed to revive 21 auxiliary and subordinate shrines whose shrines had ceased to exist. In 1874, the restoration of {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社}}, {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)}} was accomplished. In 1875, a petition was submitted to the Ministry of Religion for the restoration of five more shrines, but it was not granted. However, in 1880, Ogoso Shrine and {{ill|Ashihara Shrine|ja|葭原神社}} were reconstructed using old materials from the East and West storehouses of {{ill|Mishioden Shrine|ja|御塩殿神社}}. The remaining 15 shrines were never restored.
The shrine was reconstructed in March 1911.

{{神社 |名称 = 小社神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Ogoso-jinja (Naiku) 01.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[玉城町]]小社曽根字小社山1955 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 46.8 | 経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 38 | 経度秒 = 52.8 |祭神 = 高水上命 |社格 = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}}末社 |創建 = [[延暦]]23年（[[804年]]）以前 |本殿 = [[神明造]] |別名 = 雨の宮さん |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Ogosō Shrine''' (小社神社) is a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|摂末社}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} (Inner Shrine) within the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} complex. This shrine enshrines a {{ill|suijin|ja|水神|lt=water deity}}, and it is located in an area that was historically the base of the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏}}, who served as {{ill|negi (Shinto)|ja|禰宜}} (senior priests) at the Inner Shrine and developed the surrounding region.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing, ed. (2008), p.58.</ref>
[[Category:Branch shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[ja:小社神社]]
== Overview ==
Ogoso Shrine (小社神社) is located in Ogoso Sone, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture. It is one of the 13 auxiliary and subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine (Naiku) of Ise Grand Shrine. Among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine, it ranks 4th. The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 7,014.9 square meters. Locally, it is affectionately known as "Ame no Miya" or "Ame no Miya-san" and has been a place for rain-making prayers during droughts.
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is Takaminakami no Mikoto (高水上命), a water deity also worshipped at {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社}} within Tamaki Town. The sacred object of worship is a stone.
== History ==
Ogoso Shrine was established by Yamatohime-no-Mikoto. According to the definition of subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, it was founded before the completion of the "Enryaku Gishikicho" in 804. The Arakida clan, who developed the area as the sacred rice fields of the Inner Shrine, were based in Ogoso Sone and revered Ogoso Shrine as their clan deity. The "Kenkyu Third Year Annual Events of the Imperial Grand Shrine" (1192) records that the clan visited the shrine on the first Monkey day of the 4th lunar month.
During the Sengoku period, the Arakida clan relocated to Uji, where the Inner Shrine is located, due to the incursion of warrior factions, leading to the decline of Ogoso Shrine. In the early Meiji period, the Grand Shrine of Ise aimed to revive 21 auxiliary and subordinate shrines whose shrines had ceased to exist. In 1874, the restoration of {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社}}, {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)}} was accomplished. In 1875, a petition was submitted to the Ministry of Religion for the restoration of five more shrines, but it was not granted. However, in 1880, Ogoso Shrine and {{ill|Ashihara Shrine|ja|葭原神社}} were reconstructed using old materials from the East and West storehouses of {{ill|Mishioden Shrine|ja|御塩殿神社}}. The remaining 15 shrines were never restored.
The shrine was reconstructed in March 1911.
== Access ==
Ogoso Shrine is surrounded by rice fields and is located slightly off Mie Prefectural Route 717 Iwadetamura Line.
=== Public Transport === * Approximately a 25-minute walk southeast (about 2.0 km) from Tamura Station on the JR Sangu Line.
=== By Car === * Approximately 10 minutes (about 5.0 km) from Tamaki IC on the Ise Expressway. There is no parking available nearby.

However, in 1880, Ogoso Shrine and {{ill|Ashihara Shrine|ja|葭原神社}} were reconstructed using old materials from the East and West storehouses of {{ill|Mishioden Shrine|ja|御塩殿神社}}. The remaining 15 shrines were never restored.

== Overview ==
Ogoso Shrine (小社神社) is located in Ogoso Sone, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture. It is one of the 13 auxiliary and subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine (Naiku) of Ise Grand Shrine. Among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine, it ranks 4th. The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 7,014.9 square meters. Locally, it is affectionately known as "Ame no Miya" or "Ame no Miya-san" and has been a place for rain-making prayers during droughts.
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is Takaminakami no Mikoto (高水上命), a water deity also worshipped at {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社}} within Tamaki Town. The sacred object of worship is a stone.
== History ==
Ogoso Shrine was established by Yamatohime-no-Mikoto. According to the definition of subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, it was founded before the completion of the "Enryaku Gishikicho" in 804. The Arakida clan, who developed the area as the sacred rice fields of the Inner Shrine, were based in Ogoso Sone and revered Ogoso Shrine as their clan deity. The "Kenkyu Third Year Annual Events of the Imperial Grand Shrine" (1192) records that the clan visited the shrine on the first Monkey day of the 4th lunar month.
During the Sengoku period, the Arakida clan relocated to Uji, where the Inner Shrine is located, due to the incursion of warrior factions, leading to the decline of Ogoso Shrine. In the early Meiji period, the Grand Shrine of Ise aimed to revive 21 auxiliary and subordinate shrines whose shrines had ceased to exist. In 1874, the restoration of {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社}}, {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)}} was accomplished. In 1875, a petition was submitted to the Ministry of Religion for the restoration of five more shrines, but it was not granted. However, in 1880, Ogoso Shrine and {{ill|Ashihara Shrine|ja|葭原神社}} were reconstructed using old materials from the East and West storehouses of {{ill|Mishioden Shrine|ja|御塩殿神社}}. The remaining 15 shrines were never restored.
The shrine was reconstructed in March 1911.

Ogoso Shrine is surrounded by rice fields and is located slightly off Mie Prefectural Route 717 Iwadetamura Line.
=== Public Transport === * Approximately a 25-minute walk southeast (about 2.0 km) from Tamura Station on the JR Sangu Line.
=== By Car === * Approximately 10 minutes (about 5.0 km) from Tamaki IC on the Ise Expressway. There is no parking available nearby.

{{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:小社神社]][[ja:小社神社]][[Category:小社神社]][[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:小社神社]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:小社神社]][[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Ogosō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogosō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogosō Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogosō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[en:小社神社]]

{{神社 |名称 = 小社神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Ogoso-jinja (Naiku) 01.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[玉城町]]小社曽根字小社山1955 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 46.8 | 経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 38 | 経度秒 = 52.8 |祭神 = 高水上命 |社格 = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}}末社 |創建 = [[延暦]]23年（[[804年]]）以前 |本殿 = [[神明造]] |別名 = 雨の宮さん |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Ogosō Shrine''' (小社神社) is a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|摂末社}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} (Inner Shrine) within the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} complex. This shrine enshrines a {{ill|suijin|ja|水神|lt=water deity}}, and it is located in an area that was historically the base of the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏}}, who served as {{ill|negi (Shinto)|ja|禰宜}} (senior priests) at the Inner Shrine and developed the surrounding region.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing, ed. (2008), p.58.</ref>
[[Category:Branch shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[ja:小社神社]]
== Overview ==
Ogoso Shrine (小社神社) is located in Ogoso Sone, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture. It is one of the 13 auxiliary and subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine (Naiku) of Ise Grand Shrine. Among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine, it ranks 4th. The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 7,014.9 square meters. Locally, it is affectionately known as "Ame no Miya" or "Ame no Miya-san" and has been a place for rain-making prayers during droughts.
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is Takaminakami no Mikoto (高水上命), a water deity also worshipped at {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社}} within Tamaki Town. The sacred object of worship is a stone.
== History ==
Ogoso Shrine was established by Yamatohime-no-Mikoto. According to the definition of subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, it was founded before the completion of the "Enryaku Gishikicho" in 804. The Arakida clan, who developed the area as the sacred rice fields of the Inner Shrine, were based in Ogoso Sone and revered Ogoso Shrine as their clan deity. The "Kenkyu Third Year Annual Events of the Imperial Grand Shrine" (1192) records that the clan visited the shrine on the first Monkey day of the 4th lunar month.
During the Sengoku period, the Arakida clan relocated to Uji, where the Inner Shrine is located, due to the incursion of warrior factions, leading to the decline of Ogoso Shrine. In the early Meiji period, the Grand Shrine of Ise aimed to revive 21 auxiliary and subordinate shrines whose shrines had ceased to exist. In 1874, the restoration of {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社}}, {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)}} was accomplished. In 1875, a petition was submitted to the Ministry of Religion for the restoration of five more shrines, but it was not granted. However, in 1880, Ogoso Shrine and {{ill|Ashihara Shrine|ja|葭原神社}} were reconstructed using old materials from the East and West storehouses of {{ill|Mishioden Shrine|ja|御塩殿神社}}. The remaining 15 shrines were never restored.
The shrine was reconstructed in March 1911.
== Access ==
Ogoso Shrine is surrounded by rice fields and is located slightly off Mie Prefectural Route 717 Iwadetamura Line.
=== Public Transport === * Approximately a 25-minute walk southeast (about 2.0 km) from Tamura Station on the JR Sangu Line.
=== By Car === * Approximately 10 minutes (about 5.0 km) from Tamaki IC on the Ise Expressway. There is no parking available nearby.

[[Category:Branch shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[ja:小社神社]]
== Overview ==
Ogoso Shrine (小社神社) is located in Ogoso Sone, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture. It is one of the 13 auxiliary and subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine (Naiku) of Ise Grand Shrine. Among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine, it ranks 4th. The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 7,014.9 square meters. Locally, it is affectionately known as "Ame no Miya" or "Ame no Miya-san" and has been a place for rain-making prayers during droughts.
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is Takaminakami no Mikoto (高水上命), a water deity also worshipped at {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社}} within Tamaki Town. The sacred object of worship is a stone.
== History ==
Ogoso Shrine was established by Yamatohime-no-Mikoto. According to the definition of subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, it was founded before the completion of the "Enryaku Gishikicho" in 804. The Arakida clan, who developed the area as the sacred rice fields of the Inner Shrine, were based in Ogoso Sone and revered Ogoso Shrine as their clan deity. The "Kenkyu Third Year Annual Events of the Imperial Grand Shrine" (1192) records that the clan visited the shrine on the first Monkey day of the 4th lunar month.
During the Sengoku period, the Arakida clan relocated to Uji, where the Inner Shrine is located, due to the incursion of warrior factions, leading to the decline of Ogoso Shrine. In the early Meiji period, the Grand Shrine of Ise aimed to revive 21 auxiliary and subordinate shrines whose shrines had ceased to exist. In 1874, the restoration of {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社}}, {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)}} was accomplished. In 1875, a petition was submitted to the Ministry of Religion for the restoration of five more shrines, but it was not granted. However, in 1880, Ogoso Shrine and {{ill|Ashihara Shrine|ja|葭原神社}} were reconstructed using old materials from the East and West storehouses of {{ill|Mishioden Shrine|ja|御塩殿神社}}. The remaining 15 shrines were never restored.
The shrine was reconstructed in March 1911.

Ogoso Shrine is surrounded by rice fields and is located slightly off Mie Prefectural Route 717 Iwadetamura Line.
=== Public Transport === * Approximately a 25-minute walk southeast (about 2.0 km) from Tamura Station on the JR Sangu Line.

However, in 1880, Ogoso Shrine and {{ill|Ashihara Shrine|ja|葭原神社}} were reconstructed using old materials from the East and West storehouses of {{ill|Mishioden Shrine|ja|御塩殿神社}}. The remaining 15 shrines were never restored.
The shrine was reconstructed in March 1911.
== Access ==
Ogoso Shrine is surrounded by rice fields and is located slightly off Mie Prefectural Route 717 Iwadetamura Line.
=== Public Transport === * Approximately a 25-minute walk southeast (about 2.0 km) from Tamura Station on the JR Sangu Line.
=== By Car === * Approximately 10 minutes (about 5.0 km) from Tamaki IC on the Ise Expressway. There is no parking available nearby.

{{神社 |名称 = 小社神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Ogoso-jinja (Naiku) 01.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[玉城町]]小社曽根字小社山1955 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 46.8 | 経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 38 | 経度秒 = 52.8 |祭神 = 高水上命 |社格 = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}}末社 |創建 = [[延暦]]23年（[[804年]]）以前 |本殿 = [[神明造]] |別名 = 雨の宮さん |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Ogosō Shrine''' (小社神社) is a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|摂末社}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (Inner Shrine) within the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} complex. (2008), p.58.</ref>
[[Category:Branch shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[ja:小社神社]]
== Overview ==
Ogoso Shrine (小社神社) is located in Ogoso Sone, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture. It is one of the 13 auxiliary and subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine (Naiku) of Ise Grand Shrine. Among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine, it ranks 4th. The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 7,014.9 square meters. Locally, it is affectionately known as "Ame no Miya" or "Ame no Miya-san" and has been a place for rain-making prayers during droughts.
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is Takaminakami no Mikoto (高水上命), a water deity also worshipped at {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社}} within Tamaki Town. The sacred object of worship is a stone.
== History ==
Ogoso Shrine was established by Yamatohime-no-Mikoto. According to the definition of subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, it was founded before the completion of the "Enryaku Gishikicho" in 804. The Arakida clan, who developed the area as the sacred rice fields of the Inner Shrine, were based in Ogoso Sone and revered Ogoso Shrine as their clan deity. The "Kenkyu Third Year Annual Events of the Imperial Grand Shrine" (1192) records that the clan visited the shrine on the first Monkey day of the 4th lunar month.
During the Sengoku period, the Arakida clan relocated to Uji, where the Inner Shrine is located, due to the incursion of warrior factions, leading to the decline of Ogoso Shrine. In the early Meiji period, the Grand Shrine of Ise aimed to revive 21 auxiliary and subordinate shrines whose shrines had ceased to exist. In 1874, the restoration of {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社}}, {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)}} was accomplished. In 1875, a petition was submitted to the Ministry of Religion for the restoration of five more shrines, but it was not granted. However, in 1880, Ogoso Shrine and {{ill|Ashihara Shrine|ja|葭原神社}} were reconstructed using old materials from the East and West storehouses of {{ill|Mishioden Shrine|ja|御塩殿神社}}. The remaining 15 shrines were never restored.
The shrine was reconstructed in March 1911.
== Access ==
Ogoso Shrine is surrounded by rice fields and is located slightly off Mie Prefectural Route 717 Iwadetamura Line.
=== Public Transport === * Approximately a 25-minute walk southeast (about 2.0 km) from Tamura Station on the JR Sangu Line.
=== By Car === * Approximately 10 minutes (about 5.0 km) from Tamaki IC on the Ise Expressway. There is no parking available nearby.

{{神社 |名称 = 小社神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Ogoso-jinja (Naiku) 01.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[玉城町]]小社曽根字小社山1955 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 46.8 | 経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 38 | 経度秒 = 52.8 |祭神 = 高水上命 |社格 = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}}末社 |創建 = [[延暦]]23年（[[804年]]）以前 |本殿 = [[神明造]] |別名 = 雨の宮さん |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Ogosō Shrine''' (小社神社) is a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|摂末社}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (Inner Shrine) within the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} complex. This shrine enshrines a {{ill|suijin|ja|水神|lt=water deity}}, and it is located in an area that was historically the base of the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏}}, who served as {{ill|negi (Shinto)|ja|禰宜}} (senior priests) at the Inner Shrine and developed the surrounding region.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing, ed. (2008), p.58.</ref>
[[Category:Branch shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[ja:小社神社]]
== Overview ==
Ogoso Shrine (小社神社) is located in Ogoso Sone, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture. It is one of the 13 auxiliary and subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine (Naiku) of Ise Grand Shrine. Among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine, it ranks 4th. The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 7,014.9 square meters. Locally, it is affectionately known as "Ame no Miya" or "Ame no Miya-san" and has been a place for rain-making prayers during droughts.
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is Takaminakami no Mikoto (高水上命), a water deity also worshipped at {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社}} within Tamaki Town. The sacred object of worship is a stone.
== History ==
Ogoso Shrine was established by Yamatohime-no-Mikoto. According to the definition of subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, it was founded before the Completion of the "Enryaku Gishikicho" in 804. The Arakida clan, who developed the area as the sacred rice fields of the Inner Shrine, were based in Ogoso Sone and revered Ogoso Shrine as their clan deity. The "Kenkyu Third Year Annual Events of the Imperial Grand Shrine" (1192) records that the clan visited the shrine on the first Monkey day of the 4th lunar month.
During the Sengoku period, the Arakida clan relocated to Uji, where the Inner Shrine is located, due to the incursion of warrior factions, leading to the decline of Ogoso Shrine. In the early Meiji period, the Grand Shrine of Ise aimed to revive 21 auxiliary and subordinate shrines whose shrines had ceased to exist. In 1874, the restoration of {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社}}, {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)}} was accomplished. In 1875, a petition was submitted to the Ministry of Religion for the restoration of five more shrines, but it was not granted. However, in 1880, Ogoso Shrine and {{ill|Ashihara Shrine|ja|葭原神社}} were reconstructed using old materials from the East and West storehouses of {{ill|Mishioden Shrine|ja|御塩殿神社}}. The remaining 15 shrines were never restored.
The shrine was reconstructed in March 1911.
== Access ==
Ogoso Shrine is surrounded by rice fields and is located slightly off Mie Prefectural Route 717 Iwadetamura Line.
=== Public Transport === * Approximately a 25-minute walk southeast (about 2.0 km) from Tamura Station on the JR Sangu Line.
=== By Car === * Approximately 10 minutes (about 5.0 km) from Tamaki IC on the Ise Expressway. There is no parking available nearby.

{{神社 |名称 = 小社神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Ogoso-jinja (Naiku) 01.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[玉城町]]小社曽根字小社山1955 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 46.8 | 経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 38 | 経度秒 = 52.8 |祭神 = 高水上命 |社格 = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}}末社 |創建 = [[延暦]]23年（[[804年]]）以前 |本殿 = [[神明造]] |別名 = 雨の宮さん |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Ogosō Shrine''' (小社神社) is a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|摂末社}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (Inner Shrine) within the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} complex. This shrine enshrines a {{ill|suijin|ja|水神|lt=water deity}}, and it is located in an area that was historically the base of the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏}}, who served as {{ill|negi (Shinto)|ja|禰宜}} (senior priests) at the Inner Shrine and developed the surrounding region.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing, ed. (2008), p.58.</ref>
[[Category:Branch shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[ja:小社神社]]
== Overview ==
Ogoso Shrine (小社神社) is located in Ogoso Sone, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture. It is one of the 13 auxiliary and subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine (Naiku) of Ise Grand Shrine. Among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine, it ranks 4th. The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 7,014.9 square meters. Locally, it is affectionately known as "Ame no Miya" or "Ame no Miya-san" and has been a place for rain-making prayers during droughts.
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is Takaminakami no Mikoto (高水上命), a water deity also worshipped at {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社}} within Tamaki Town. The sacred object of worship is a stone.
== History ==
Ogoso Shrine was established by Yamatohime-no-Mikoto. According to the definition of subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, it was founded before the Completion of the "Enryaku Gishikicho" in 804. The Arakida clan, who developed the area as the sacred rice fields of the Inner Shrine, were based in Ogoso Sone and revered Ogoso Shrine as their clan deity. The "Kenkyu Third Year Annual Events of the Imperial Grand Shrine" (1192) records that the clan visited the shrine on the first Monkey day of the 4th lunar month.
During the Sengoku period, the Arakida clan relocated to Uji, where the Inner Shrine is located, due to the incursion of warrior factions, leading to the decline of Ogoso Shrine. In the early Meiji period, the Grand Shrine of Ise aimed to revive 21 auxiliary and subordinate shrines whose shrines had ceased to exist. In 1874, the restoration of {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社}}, {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)}} was accomplished. In 1875, a petition was submitted to the Ministry of Religion for the restoration of five more shrines, but it was not granted. However, in 1880, Ogoso Shrine and {{ill|Ashihara Shrine|ja|葭原神社}} were reconstructed using old materials from the East and West storehouses of {{ill|Mishioden Shrine|ja|御塩殿神社}}. The remaining 15 shrines were never restored.
The shrine was reconstructed in March 1911.
== Access ==
Ogoso Shrine is surrounded by rice fields and is located slightly off Mie Prefectural Route 717 Iwadetamura Line.
=== Public Transport === * Approximately a 25-minute walk southeast (about 2.0 km) from Tamura Station on the JR Sangu Line.
=== By Car === * Approximately 10 minutes (about 5.0 km) from Tamaki IC on the Ise Expressway. There is no parking available nearby.

{{神社 |名称 = 小社神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Ogoso-jinja (Naiku) 01.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[玉城町]]小社曽根字小社山1955 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 46.8 | 経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 38 | 経度秒 = 52.8 |祭神 = 高水上命 |社格 = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}}末社 |創建 = [[延暦]]23年（[[804]]）以前 |本殿 = [[神明造]] |別名 = 雨の宮さん |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Ogosō Shrine''' (小社神社) is a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|摂末社}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (Inner Shrine) within the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} complex. This shrine enshrines a {{ill|suijin|ja|水神|lt=water deity}}, and it is located in an area that was historically the base of the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏}}, who served as {{ill|negi (Shinto)|ja|禰宜}} (senior priests) at the Inner Shrine and developed the surrounding region.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing, ed. (2008), p.58.</ref>
[[Category:Branch shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[ja:小社神社]]
== Overview ==
Ogoso Shrine (小社神社) is located in Ogoso Sone, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture. It is one of the 13 auxiliary and subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine (Naiku) of Ise Grand Shrine. Among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine, it ranks 4th. The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 7,014.9 square meters. Locally, it is affectionately known as "Ame no Miya" or "Ame no Miya-san" and has been a place for rain-making prayers during droughts.
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is Takaminakami no Mikoto (高水上命), a water deity also worshipped at {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社}} within Tamaki Town. The sacred object of worship is a stone.
== History ==
Ogoso Shrine was established by Yamatohime-no-Mikoto. According to the definition of subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, it was founded before the Completion of the "Enryaku Gishikicho" in 804. The Arakida clan, who developed the area as the sacred rice fields of the Inner Shrine, were based in Ogoso Sone and revered Ogoso Shrine as their clan deity. The "Kenkyu Third Year Annual Events of the Imperial Grand Shrine" (1192) records that the clan visited the shrine on the first Monkey day of the 4th lunar month.
During the Sengoku period, the Arakida clan relocated to Uji, where the Inner Shrine is located, due to the incursion of warrior factions, leading to the decline of Ogoso Shrine. In the early Meiji period, the Grand Shrine of Ise aimed to revive 21 auxiliary and subordinate shrines whose shrines had ceased to exist. In 1874, the restoration of {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社}}, {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)}} was accomplished. In 1875, a petition was submitted to the Ministry of Religion for the restoration of five more shrines, but it was not granted. However, in 1880, Ogoso Shrine and {{ill|Ashihara Shrine|ja|葭原神社}} were reconstructed using old materials from the East and West storehouses of {{ill|Mishioden Shrine|ja|御塩殿神社}}. The remaining 15 shrines were never restored.
The shrine was reconstructed in March 1911.
== Access ==
Ogoso Shrine is surrounded by rice fields and is located slightly off Mie Prefectural Route 717 Iwadetamura Line.
=== Public Transport === * Approximately a 25-minute walk southeast (about 2.0 km) from Tamura Station on the JR Sangu Line.
=== By Car === * Approximately 10 minutes (about 5.0 km) from Tamaki IC on the Ise Expressway. There is no parking available nearby.
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}

ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第3巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第92集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和59年3月10日、308p. * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3年11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013年11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7

The enshrined deity is Takaminakami no Mikoto (高水上命), a water deity also worshipped at {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社}} within Tamaki Town. The sacred object of worship is a stone.
== History ==
Ogoso Shrine was established by Yamatohime-no-Mikoto. According to the definition of subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, it was founded before the Completion of the "Enryaku Gishikicho" in 804. The Arakida clan, who developed the area as the sacred rice fields of the Inner Shrine, were based in Ogoso Sone and revered Ogoso Shrine as their clan deity. The "Kenkyu Third Year Annual Events of the Imperial Grand Shrine" (1192) records that the clan visited the shrine on the first Monkey day of the 4th lunar month.
During the Sengoku period, the Arakida clan relocated to Uji, where the Inner Shrine is located, due to the incursion of warrior factions, leading to the decline of Ogoso Shrine. In the early Meiji period, the Grand Shrine of Ise aimed to revive 21 auxiliary and subordinate shrines whose shrines had ceased to exist. In 1874, the restoration of {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社}}, {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)}} was accomplished. In 1875, a petition was submitted to the Ministry of Religion for the restoration of five more shrines, but it was not granted. However, in 1880, Ogoso Shrine and {{ill|Ashihara Shrine|ja|葭原神社}} were reconstructed using old materials from the East and West storehouses of {{ill|Mishioden Shrine|ja|御塩殿神社}}. The remaining 15 shrines were never restored.

{{神社 |名称 = 小社神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Ogoso-jinja (Naiku) 01.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[玉城町]]小社曽根字小社山1955 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 46.8 | 経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 38 | 経度秒 = 52.8 |祭神 = 高水上命 |社格 = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}}末社 |創建 = [[延暦]]23年（[[804]]）以前 |本殿 = [[神明造]] |別名 = 雨の宮さん |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Ogosō Shrine''' (小社神社) is a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|摂末社}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (Inner Shrine) within the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} complex. This shrine enshrines a {{ill|suijin|ja|水神|lt=water deity}}, and it is located in an area that was historically the base of the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏}}, who served as {{ill|negi (Shinto)|ja|禰宜}} (senior priests) at the Inner Shrine and developed the surrounding region.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing, ed. (2008), p.58.</ref>
[[Category:Branch shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[ja:小社神社]]
== Overview ==
Ogoso Shrine (小社神社) is located in Ogoso Sone, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture. It is one of the 13 auxiliary and subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine (Naiku) of Ise Grand Shrine. Among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine, it ranks 4th. The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 7,014.9 square meters. Locally, it is affectionately known as "Ame no Miya" or "Ame no Miya-san" and has been a place for rain-making prayers during droughts.
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is Takaminakami no Mikoto (高水上命), a water deity also worshipped at {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社}} within Tamaki Town. The sacred object of worship is a stone.
== History ==
Ogoso Shrine was established by Yamatohime-no-Mikoto. According to the definition of subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, it was founded before the Completion of the "Enryaku Gishikicho" in 804. The Arakida clan, who developed the area as the sacred rice fields of the Inner Shrine, were based in Ogoso Sone and revered Ogoso Shrine as their clan deity. The "Kenkyu Third Year Annual Events of the Imperial Grand Shrine" (1192) records that the clan visited the shrine on the first Monkey day of the 4th lunar month.
During the Sengoku period, the Arakida clan relocated to Uji, where the Inner Shrine is located, due to the incursion of warrior factions, leading to the decline of Ogoso Shrine. In the early Meiji period, the Grand Shrine of Ise aimed to revive 21 auxiliary and subordinate shrines whose shrines had ceased to exist. In 1874, the restoration of {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社}}, {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)}} was accomplished. In 1875, a petition was submitted to the Ministry of Religion for the restoration of five more shrines, but it was not granted. However, in 1880, Ogoso Shrine and {{ill|Ashihara Shrine|ja|葭原神社}} were reconstructed using old materials from the East and West storehouses of {{ill|Mishioden Shrine|ja|御塩殿神社}}. The remaining 15 shrines were never restored.
The shrine was reconstructed in March 1911.
== Access ==
Ogoso Shrine is surrounded by rice fields and is located slightly off Mie Prefectural Route 717 Iwadetamura Line.
=== Public Transport === * Approximately a 25-minute walk southeast (about 2.0 km) from Tamura Station on the JR Sangu Line.
=== By Car === * Approximately 10 minutes (about 5.0 km) from Tamaki IC on the Ise Expressway. There is no parking available nearby.

{{神社 |名称 = 小社神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Ogoso-jinja (Naiku) 01.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[玉城町]]小社曽根字小社山1955 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 46.8 | 経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 38 | 経度秒 = 52.8 |祭神 = 高水上命 |社格 = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}}末社 |創建 = [[延暦]]23年（[[804]]）以前 |本殿 = [[神明造]] |別名 = 雨の宮さん |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Ogosō Shrine''' (小社神社) is a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|摂末社}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (Inner Shrine) within the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} complex. This shrine enshrines a {{ill|suijin|ja|水神|lt=water deity}}, and it is located in an area that was historically the base of the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏}}, who served as {{ill|negi (Shinto)|ja|禰宜}} (senior priests) at the Inner Shrine and developed the surrounding region.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing, ed. (2008), p.58.</ref>
[[Category:Branch shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[ja:小社神社]]
== Overview ==
Ogoso Shrine (小社神社) is located in Ogoso Sone, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture. It is one of the 13 auxiliary and subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine (Naiku) of Ise Grand Shrine. Among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine, it ranks 4th. The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 7,014.9 square meters. Locally, it is affectionately known as "Ame no Miya" or "Ame no Miya-san" and has been a place for rain-making prayers during droughts.
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is Takaminakami no Mikoto (高水上命), a water deity also worshipped at {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社}} within Tamaki Town. The sacred object of worship is a stone.
== History ==
Ogoso Shrine was established by Yamatohime-no-Mikoto. According to the definition of subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, it was founded before the Completion of the "Enryaku Gishikicho" in 804. The Arakida clan, who developed the area as the sacred rice fields of the Inner Shrine, were based in Ogoso Sone and revered Ogoso Shrine as their clan deity. The "Kenkyu Third Year Annual Events of the Imperial Grand Shrine" (1192) records that the clan visited the shrine on the first Monkey day of the 4th lunar month.
During the Sengoku period, the Arakida clan relocated to Uji, where the Inner Shrine is located, due to the incursion of warrior factions, leading to the decline of Ogoso Shrine. In the early Meiji period, the Grand Shrine of Ise aimed to revive 21 auxiliary and subordinate shrines whose shrines had ceased to exist. In 1874, the restoration of {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社}}, {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)}} was accomplished. In 1875, a petition was submitted to the Ministry of Religion for the restoration of five more shrines, but it was not granted. However, in 1880, Ogoso Shrine and {{ill|Ashihara Shrine|ja|葭原神社}} were reconstructed using old materials from the East and West storehouses of {{ill|Mishioden Shrine|ja|御塩殿神社}}. The remaining 15 shrines were never restored.
The shrine was reconstructed in March 1911.
== Access ==
Ogoso Shrine is surrounded by rice fields and is located slightly off Mie Prefectural Route 717 Iwadetamura Line.
=== Public Transport === * Approximately a 25-minute walk southeast (about 2.0 km) from Tamura Station on the JR Sangu Line.
=== By Car === * Approximately 10 minutes (about 5.0 km) from Tamaki IC on the Ise Expressway. There is no parking available nearby.

{{神社 |名称 = 小社神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Ogoso-jinja (Naiku) 01.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[玉城町]]小社曽根字小社山1955 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 46.8 | 経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 38 | 経度秒 = 52.8 |祭神 = 高水上命 |社格 = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}}末社 |創建 = [[延暦]]23年（[[804]]）以前 |本殿 = [[神明造]] |別名 = 雨の宮さん |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Ogosō Shrine''' (小社神社) is a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|摂末社}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (Inner Shrine) within the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} complex. This shrine enshrines a {{ill|suijin|ja|水神|lt=water deity}}, and it is located in an area that was historically the base of the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏}}, who served as {{ill|negi (Shinto)|ja|禰宜}} (senior priests) at the Inner Shrine and developed the surrounding region.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing, ed. (2008), p.58.</ref>
[[Category:Branch shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[ja:小社神社]]
== Overview ==
Ogoso Shrine (小社神社) is located in Ogoso Sone, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture. It is one of the 13 auxiliary and subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine (Naiku) of Ise Grand Shrine. Among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine, it ranks 4th. The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 7,014.9 square meters. Locally, it is affectionately known as "Ame no Miya" or "Ame no Miya-san" and has been a place for rain-making prayers during droughts.
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is Takaminakami no Mikoto (高水上命), a water deity also worshipped at {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社}} within Tamaki Town. The sacred object of worship is a stone.
== History ==
Ogoso Shrine was established by Yamatohime-no-Mikoto. According to the definition of subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, it was founded before the Completion tier 4]][[Category: of the "Enryaku Gishikicho" in 804. The Arakida clan, who developed the area as the sacred rice fields of the Inner Shrine, were based in Ogoso Sone and revered Ogoso Shrine as their clan deity. The "Kenkyu Third Year Annual Events of the Imperial Grand Shrine" (1192) records that the clan visited the shrine on the first Monkey day of the 4th lunar month.
During the Sengoku period, the Arakida clan relocated to Uji, where the Inner Shrine is located, due to the incursion of warrior factions, leading to the decline of Ogoso Shrine. In the early Meiji period, the Grand Shrine of Ise aimed to revive 21 auxiliary and subordinate shrines whose shrines had ceased to exist. In 1874, the restoration of {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社}}, {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)}} was accomplished. In 1875, a petition was submitted to the Ministry of Religion for the restoration of five more shrines, but it was not granted. However, in 1880, Ogoso Shrine and {{ill|Ashihara Shrine|ja|葭原神社}} were reconstructed using old materials from the East and West storehouses of {{ill|Mishioden Shrine|ja|御塩殿神社}}. The remaining 15 shrines were never restored.
The shrine was reconstructed in March 1911.
== Access ==
Ogoso Shrine is surrounded by rice fields and is located slightly off Mie Prefectural Route 717 Iwadetamura Line.
=== Public Transport === * Approximately a 25-minute walk southeast (about 2.0 km) from Tamura Station on the JR Sangu Line.
=== By Car === * Approximately 10 minutes (about 5.0 km) from Tamaki IC on the Ise Expressway. There is no parking available nearby.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "== See Also ==" to " == See Also! =="
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 小社神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Ogoso-jinja (Naiku) 01.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[玉城町]]小社曽根字小社山1955 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 46.8 | 経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 38 | 経度秒 = 52.8 |祭神 = 高水上命 |社格 = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}}末社 |創建 = [[延暦]]23年（[[804]]）以前 |本殿 = [[神明造]] |別名 = 雨の宮さん |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Ogosō Shrine''' (小社神社) is a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|摂末社}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (Inner Shrine) within the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} complex. This shrine enshrines a {{ill|suijin|ja|水神|lt=water deity}}, and it is located in an area that was historically the base of the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏}}, who served as {{ill|negi (Shinto)|ja|禰宜}} (senior priests) at the Inner Shrine and developed the surrounding region.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing, ed. (2008), p.58.</ref>
[[Category:Branch shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[ja:小社神社]]
== Overview ==
Ogoso Shrine (小社神社) is located in Ogoso Sone, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture. It is one of the 13 auxiliary and subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine (Naiku) of Ise Grand Shrine. Among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine, it ranks 4th. The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 7,014.9 square meters. Locally, it is affectionately known as "Ame no Miya" or "Ame no Miya-san" and has been a place for rain-making prayers during droughts.
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is Takaminakami no Mikoto (高水上命), a water deity also worshipped at {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社}} within Tamaki Town. The sacred object of worship is a stone.
== History ==
Ogoso Shrine was established by Yamatohime-no-Mikoto. According to the definition of subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, it was founded before the Completion tier 4]][[Category: of the "Enryaku Gishikicho" in 804. The Arakida clan, who developed the area as the sacred rice fields of the Inner Shrine, were based in Ogoso Sone and revered Ogoso Shrine as their clan deity. The "Kenkyu Third Year Annual Events of the Imperial Grand Shrine" (1192) records that the clan visited the shrine on the first Monkey day of the 4th lunar month.
During the Sengoku period, the Arakida clan relocated to Uji, where the Inner Shrine is located, due to the incursion of warrior factions, leading to the decline of Ogoso Shrine. In the early Meiji period, the Grand Shrine of Ise aimed to revive 21 auxiliary and subordinate shrines whose shrines had ceased to exist. In 1874, the restoration of {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社}}, {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)}} was accomplished. In 1875, a petition was submitted to the Ministry of Religion for the restoration of five more shrines, but it was not granted. However, in 1880, Ogoso Shrine and {{ill|Ashihara Shrine|ja|葭原神社}} were reconstructed using old materials from the East and West storehouses of {{ill|Mishioden Shrine|ja|御塩殿神社}}. The remaining 15 shrines were never restored.
The shrine was reconstructed in March 1911.
== Access ==
Ogoso Shrine is surrounded by rice fields and is located slightly off Mie Prefectural Route 717 Iwadetamura Line.
=== Public Transport === * Approximately a 25-minute walk southeast (about 2.0 km) from Tamura Station on the JR Sangu Line.
=== By Car === * Approximately 10 minutes (about 5.0 km) from Tamaki IC on the Ise Expressway. There is no parking available nearby.
== References ==
{{Reflist|2}}

ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第3巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第92集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和59年3月10日、308p. * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3年11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013年11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also! == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * [[下外城田村]]

{{神社 |名称 = 小社神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Ogoso-jinja (Naiku) 01.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[玉城町]]小社曽根字小社山1955 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 46.8 | 経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 38 | 経度秒 = 52.8 |祭神 = 高水上命 |社格 = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}}末社 |創建 = [[延暦]]23rd year （[[804]]）以前 |本殿 = [[神明造]] |別名 = 雨の宮さん |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Ogosō Shrine''' (小社神社) is a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|摂末社}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (Inner Shrine) within the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} complex. This shrine enshrines a {{ill|suijin|ja|水神|lt=water deity}}, and it is located in an area that was historically the base of the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏}}, who served as {{ill|negi (Shinto)|ja|禰宜}} (senior priests) at the Inner Shrine and developed the surrounding region.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing, ed. (2008), p.58.</ref>
[[Category:Branch shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[ja:小社神社]]
== Overview ==
Ogoso Shrine (小社神社) is located in Ogoso Sone, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture. It is one of the 13 auxiliary and subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine (Naiku) of Ise Grand Shrine. Among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine, it ranks 4th. The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 7,014.9 square meters. Locally, it is affectionately known as "Ame no Miya" or "Ame no Miya-san" and has been a place for rain-making prayers during droughts.
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is Takaminakami no Mikoto (高水上命), a water deity also worshipped at {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社}} within Tamaki Town. The sacred object of worship is a stone.
== History ==
Ogoso Shrine was established by Yamatohime-no-Mikoto. According to the definition of subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, it was founded before the Completion tier 4]][[Category: of the "Enryaku Gishikicho" in 804. The Arakida clan, who developed the area as the sacred rice fields of the Inner Shrine, were based in Ogoso Sone and revered Ogoso Shrine as their clan deity. The "Kenkyu Third Year Annual Events of the Imperial Grand Shrine" (1192) records that the clan visited the shrine on the first Monkey day of the 4th lunar month.
During the Sengoku period, the Arakida clan relocated to Uji, where the Inner Shrine is located, due to the incursion of warrior factions, leading to the decline of Ogoso Shrine. In the early Meiji period, the Grand Shrine of Ise aimed to revive 21 auxiliary and subordinate shrines whose shrines had ceased to exist. In 1874, the restoration of {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社}}, {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)}} was accomplished. In 1875, a petition was submitted to the Ministry of Religion for the restoration of five more shrines, but it was not granted. However, in 1880, Ogoso Shrine and {{ill|Ashihara Shrine|ja|葭原神社}} were reconstructed using old materials from the East and West storehouses of {{ill|Mishioden Shrine|ja|御塩殿神社}}. The remaining 15 shrines were never restored.
The shrine was reconstructed in March 1911.
== Access ==
Ogoso Shrine is surrounded by rice fields and is located slightly off Mie Prefectural Route 717 Iwadetamura Line.
=== Public Transport === * Approximately a 25-minute walk southeast (about 2.0 km) from Tamura Station on the JR Sangu Line.
=== By Car === * Approximately 10 minutes (about 5.0 km) from Tamaki IC on the Ise Expressway. There is no parking available nearby.

{{神社 |名称 = 小社神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Ogoso-jinja (Naiku) 01.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[玉城町]]小社曽根字小社山1955 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 46.8 | 経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 38 | 経度秒 = 52.8 |祭神 = 高水上命 |社格 = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}}末社 |創建 = [[延暦]]23rd year （[[804]]）以前 |本殿 = [[神明造]] |別名 = 雨の宮さん |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Ogosō Shrine''' (小社神社) is a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|摂末社}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (Inner Shrine) within the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} complex. This shrine enshrines a {{ill|suijin|ja|水神|lt=water deity}}, and it is located in an area that was historically the base of the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏}}, who served as {{ill|negi (Shinto)|ja|禰宜}} (senior priests) at the Inner Shrine and developed the surrounding region.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing, ed. (2008), p.58.</ref>
[[Category:Branch shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[ja:小社神社]]
== Overview ==
Ogoso Shrine (小社神社) is located in Ogoso Sone, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture. It is one of the 13 auxiliary and subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine (Naiku) of Ise Grand Shrine. Among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine, it ranks 4th. The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 7,014.9 square meters. Locally, it is affectionately known as "Ame no Miya" or "Ame no Miya-san" and has been a place for rain-making prayers during droughts.
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is Takaminakami no Mikoto (高水上命), a water deity also worshipped at {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社}} within Tamaki Town. The sacred object of worship is a stone.
== History ==
Ogoso Shrine was established by Yamatohime-no-Mikoto. According to the definition of subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, it was founded before the Completion tier 4]][[Category: of the "Enryaku Gishikicho" in 804. The Arakida clan, who developed the area as the sacred rice fields of the Inner Shrine, were based in Ogoso Sone and revered Ogoso Shrine as their clan deity. The "Kenkyu Third Year Annual Events of the Imperial Grand Shrine" (1192) records that the clan visited the shrine on the first Monkey day of the 4th lunar month.
During the Sengoku period, the Arakida clan relocated to Uji, where the Inner Shrine is located, due to the incursion of warrior factions, leading to the decline of Ogoso Shrine. In the early Meiji period, the Grand Shrine of Ise aimed to revive 21 auxiliary and subordinate shrines whose shrines had ceased to exist. In 1874, the restoration of {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社}}, {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)}} was accomplished. In 1875, a petition was submitted to the Ministry of Religion for the restoration of five more shrines, but it was not granted. However, in 1880, Ogoso Shrine and {{ill|Ashihara Shrine|ja|葭原神社}} were reconstructed using old materials from the East and West storehouses of {{ill|Mishioden Shrine|ja|御塩殿神社}}. The remaining 15 shrines were never restored.
The shrine was reconstructed in March 1911.
== Access ==
Ogoso Shrine is surrounded by rice fields and is located slightly off Mie Prefectural Route 717 Iwadetamura Line.
=== Public Transport === * Approximately a 25-minute walk southeast (about 2.0 km) from Tamura Station on the JR Sangu Line.
=== By Car === * Approximately 10 minutes (about 5.0 km) from Tamaki IC on the Ise Expressway. There is no parking available nearby.

{{神社 |名称 = 小社神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Ogoso-jinja (Naiku) 01.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[玉城町]]小社曽根字小社山1955 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 46.8 | 経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 38 | 経度秒 = 52.8 |祭神 = 高水上命 |社格 = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft}}末社 |創建 = [[延暦]]23rd year （[[804]]）以前 |本殿 = [[神明造]] |別名 = 雨の宮さん |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Ogosō Shrine''' (小社神社) is a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|摂末社|12=draft}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft}} (Inner Shrine) within the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft}} complex. This shrine enshrines a {{ill|suijin|ja|水神|lt=water deity|12=draft}}, and it is located in an area that was historically the base of the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏|12=draft}}, who served as {{ill|negi (Shinto)|ja|禰宜|12=draft}} (senior priests) at the Inner Shrine and developed the surrounding region.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing, ed. (2008), p.58.</ref>
== Overview ==
Ogoso Shrine (小社神社) is located in Ogoso Sone, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture. It is one of the 13 auxiliary and subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine (Naiku) of Ise Grand Shrine. Among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine, it ranks 4th. The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 7,014.9 square meters. Locally, it is affectionately known as "Ame no Miya" or "Ame no Miya-san" and has been a place for rain-making prayers during droughts.
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is Takaminakami no Mikoto (高水上命), a water deity also worshipped at {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社|12=draft}} within Tamaki Town. The sacred object of worship is a stone.
== History ==
Ogoso Shrine was established by Yamatohime-no-Mikoto. According to the definition of subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, it was founded before the Completion tier 4]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第3巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第92集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和59th year 3月10日、308p. * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * [[下外城田村]]
== External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area04/area04_07.jpg 小社神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m08/0804.html 小社神社（おごそじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ 産業観光神社めぐり] - 三重県玉城町
{{125 Shrines of Ise}}
{{draft categories| [[Category:Branch shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category: of the "Enryaku Gishikicho" in 804. The Arakida clan, who developed the area as the sacred rice fields of the Inner Shrine, were based in Ogoso Sone and revered Ogoso Shrine as their clan deity. The "Kenkyu Third Year Annual Events of the Imperial Grand Shrine" (1192) records that the clan visited the shrine on the first Monkey day of the 4th lunar month.
During the Sengoku period, the Arakida clan relocated to Uji, where the Inner Shrine is located, due to the incursion of warrior factions, leading to the decline of Ogoso Shrine. In the early Meiji period, the Grand Shrine of Ise aimed to revive 21 auxiliary and subordinate shrines whose shrines had ceased to exist. In 1874, the restoration of {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|12=draft}} was accomplished. In 1875, a petition was submitted to the Ministry of Religion for the restoration of five more shrines, but it was not granted. However, in 1880, Ogoso Shrine and {{ill|Ashihara Shrine|ja|葭原神社|12=draft}} were reconstructed using old materials from the East and West storehouses of {{ill|Mishioden Shrine|ja|御塩殿神社|12=draft}}. The remaining 15 shrines were never restored.
The shrine was reconstructed in March 1911.
== Access ==
Ogoso Shrine is surrounded by rice fields and is located slightly off Mie Prefectural Route 717 Iwadetamura Line.
=== Public Transport === * Approximately a 25-minute walk southeast (about 2.0 km) from Tamura Station on the JR Sangu Line.
=== By Car === * Approximately 10 minutes (about 5.0 km) from Tamaki IC on the Ise Expressway. There is no parking available nearby.

== External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area04/area04_07.jpg 小社神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m08/0804.html 小社神社（おごそじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ 産業観光神社めぐり] - 三重県玉城町 {{125 Shrines of Ise}}
{{draft categories| [[Category:Branch shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category: of the "Enryaku Gishikicho" in 804. The Arakida clan, who developed the area as the sacred rice fields of the Inner Shrine, were based in Ogoso Sone and revered Ogoso Shrine as their clan deity. The "Kenkyu Third Year Annual Events of the Imperial Grand Shrine" (1192) records that the clan visited the shrine on the first Monkey day of the 4th lunar month.
During the Sengoku period, the Arakida clan relocated to Uji, where the Inner Shrine is located, due to the incursion of warrior factions, leading to the decline of Ogoso Shrine. In the early Meiji period, the Grand Shrine of Ise aimed to revive 21 auxiliary and subordinate shrines whose shrines had ceased to exist. In 1874, the restoration of {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|12=draft}} was accomplished. In 1875, a petition was submitted to the Ministry of Religion for the restoration of five more shrines, but it was not granted. However, in 1880, Ogoso Shrine and {{ill|Ashihara Shrine|ja|葭原神社|12=draft}} were reconstructed using old materials from the East and West storehouses of {{ill|Mishioden Shrine|ja|御塩殿神社|12=draft}}. The remaining 15 shrines were never restored.
The shrine was reconstructed in March 1911.
== Access ==
Ogoso Shrine is surrounded by rice fields and is located slightly off Mie Prefectural Route 717 Iwadetamura Line.
=== Public Transport === * Approximately a 25-minute walk southeast (about 2.0 km) from Tamura Station on the JR Sangu Line.
=== By Car === * Approximately 10 minutes (about 5.0 km) from Tamaki IC on the Ise Expressway. There is no parking available nearby. == References ==

== Overview ==
Ogoso Shrine (小社神社) is located in Ogoso Sone, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture. It is one of the 13 auxiliary and subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine (Naiku) of Ise Grand Shrine. Among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine, it ranks 4th. The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 7,014.9 square meters. Locally, it is affectionately known as "Ame no Miya" or "Ame no Miya-san" and has been a place for rain-making prayers during droughts.
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is Takaminakami no Mikoto (高水上命), a water deity also worshipped at {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社|12=draft}} within Tamaki Town. The sacred object of worship is a stone.
== History ==
Ogoso Shrine was established by Yamatohime-no-Mikoto. According to the definition of subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, it was founded before the Completion tier 4]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. == Overview ==
Ogoso Shrine (小社神社) is located in Ogoso Sone, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture. It is one of the 13 auxiliary and subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine (Naiku) of Ise Grand Shrine. Among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine, it ranks 4th. The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 7,014.9 square meters. Locally, it is affectionately known as "Ame no Miya" or "Ame no Miya-san" and has been a place for rain-making prayers during droughts.
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is Takaminakami no Mikoto (高水上命), a water deity also worshipped at {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社|12=draft}} within Tamaki Town. The sacred object of worship is a stone.
== History ==
Ogoso Shrine was established by Yamatohime-no-Mikoto. According to the definition of subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, it was founded before the Completion tier 4]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * [[下外城田村]]
== External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area04/area04_07.jpg 小社神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m08/0804.html 小社神社（おごそじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ 産業観光神社めぐり] - 三重県玉城町 {{125 Shrines of Ise}}
{{draft categories| [[Category:Branch shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category: of the "Enryaku Gishikicho" in 804. The Arakida clan, who developed the area as the sacred rice fields of the Inner Shrine, were based in Ogoso Sone and revered Ogoso Shrine as their clan deity. The "Kenkyu Third Year Annual Events of the Imperial Grand Shrine" (1192) records that the clan visited the shrine on the first Monkey day of the 4th lunar month.
During the Sengoku period, the Arakida clan relocated to Uji, where the Inner Shrine is located, due to the incursion of warrior factions, leading to the decline of Ogoso Shrine. In the early Meiji period, the Grand Shrine of Ise aimed to revive 21 auxiliary and subordinate shrines whose shrines had ceased to exist. In 1874, the restoration of {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|12=draft}} was accomplished. In 1875, a petition was submitted to the Ministry of Religion for the restoration of five more shrines, but it was not granted. However, in 1880, Ogoso Shrine and {{ill|Ashihara Shrine|ja|葭原神社|12=draft}} were reconstructed using old materials from the East and West storehouses of {{ill|Mishioden Shrine|ja|御塩殿神社|12=draft}}. The remaining 15 shrines were never restored.
The shrine was reconstructed in March 1911.
== Access ==
Ogoso Shrine is surrounded by rice fields and is located slightly off Mie Prefectural Route 717 Iwadetamura Line.
=== Public Transport === * Approximately a 25-minute walk southeast (about 2.0 km) from Tamura Station on the JR Sangu Line.
=== By Car === * Approximately 10 minutes (about 5.0 km) from Tamaki IC on the Ise Expressway. There is no parking available nearby. == References ==

Ogoso Shrine (小社神社) is located in Ogoso Sone, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture. It is one of the 13 auxiliary and subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine (Naiku) of Ise Grand Shrine. Among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine, it ranks 4th. The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 7,014.9 square meters. Locally, it is affectionately known as "Ame no Miya" or "Ame no Miya-san" and has been a place for rain-making prayers during droughts.

Ogoso Shrine was established by Yamatohime-no-Mikoto. According to the definition of subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, it was founded before the Completion tier 4]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第3巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第92集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和59th year 3月10日、308p. * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * [[下外城田村]]
== External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area04/area04_07.jpg 小社神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m08/0804.html 小社神社（おごそじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ 産業観光神社めぐり] - 三重県玉城町 {{125 Shrines of Ise}}
{{draft categories|
}}
{{draft categories| [[Category:Branch shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category: of the "Enryaku Gishikicho" in 804. The Arakida clan, who developed the area as the sacred rice fields of the Inner Shrine, were based in Ogoso Sone and revered Ogoso Shrine as their clan deity. The "Kenkyu Third Year Annual Events of the Imperial Grand Shrine" (1192) records that the clan visited the shrine on the first Monkey day of the 4th lunar month.
During the Sengoku period, the Arakida clan relocated to Uji, where the Inner Shrine is located, due to the incursion of warrior factions, leading to the decline of Ogoso Shrine. In the early Meiji period, the Grand Shrine of Ise aimed to revive 21 auxiliary and subordinate shrines whose shrines had ceased to exist. In 1874, the restoration of {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|12=draft|12=draft}} was accomplished. In 1875, a petition was submitted to the Ministry of Religion for the restoration of five more shrines, but it was not granted. However, in 1880, Ogoso Shrine and {{ill|Ashihara Shrine|ja|葭原神社|12=draft|12=draft}} were reconstructed using old materials from the East and West storehouses of {{ill|Mishioden Shrine|ja|御塩殿神社|12=draft|12=draft}}. The remaining 15 shrines were never restored.
The shrine was reconstructed in March 1911.
== Access ==
Ogoso Shrine is surrounded by rice fields and is located slightly off Mie Prefectural Route 717 Iwadetamura Line.
=== Public Transport === * Approximately a 25-minute walk southeast (about 2.0 km) from Tamura Station on the JR Sangu Line.
=== By Car === * Approximately 10 minutes (about 5.0 km) from Tamaki IC on the Ise Expressway. There is no parking available nearby. == References ==

{{神社 |名称 = 小社神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Ogoso-jinja (Naiku) 01.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[玉城町]]小社曽根字小社山1955 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 46.8 | 経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 38 | 経度秒 = 52.8 |祭神 = 高水上命 |社格 = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}末社 |創建 = [[延暦]]23rd year （[[804]]）以前 |本殿 = [[神明造]] |別名 = 雨の宮さん |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Ogosō Shrine''' (小社神社) is a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|摂末社|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (Inner Shrine) within the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} complex. This shrine enshrines a {{ill|suijin|ja|水神|1=Suijin|lt=water god|lt=water deity|12=draft|12=draft}}, and it is located in an area that was historically the base of the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, who served as {{ill|negi (Shinto)|ja|禰宜|12=draft|12=draft}} (senior priests) at the Inner Shrine and developed the surrounding region.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing, ed. (2008), p.58.</ref>
== Overview ==
Ogoso Shrine (小社神社) is located in Ogoso Sone, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture. It is one of the 13 auxiliary and subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine (Naiku) of Ise Grand Shrine. Among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine, it Ranks 4th. The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 7,014.9 square meters. Locally, it is affectionately known as "Ame no Miya" or "Ame no Miya-san" and has been a place for rain-making prayers during droughts.
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is Takaminakami no Mikoto (高水上命), a water deity also worshipped at {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社|12=draft|12=draft}} within Tamaki Town. The sacred object of worship is a stone.
== History ==
Ogoso Shrine was established by Yamatohime-no-Mikoto. revision.comment: 8 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:小社神社 up to rev 98019726
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 小社神社 |画像 = [[File:Ogoso-jinja (Naiku) 01.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[玉城町]]小社曽根字小社山1955 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 46.8 | 経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 38 | 経度秒 = 52.8 |祭神 = 高水上命 |社格 = [[皇大神宮]]末社 |創建 = [[延暦]]23年（[[804年]]）以前 |本殿 = [[神明造]] |別名 = 雨の宮さん |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = [[神嘗祭]] }} '''小社神社'''（おごそじんじゃ）は、[[伊勢神宮]][[皇大神宮]]（内宮）の[[摂末社|末社]]。[[水神|水の神]]を祀る神社で、内宮の[[禰宜]]を務め鎮座地周辺を開拓した[[荒木田氏]]が本拠を置いていた地にある<ref name="ic">伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：58ページ</ref>。
== 概要 == [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[玉城町]]小社曽根（おごそそね）に鎮座する<ref name="ic"/>。玉城町にある13の内宮摂末社のうちの1社である。内宮の末社16社のうち第4位である<ref>宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：13 - 14ページ</ref>。社地の面積は7反22歩（≒7,014.9[[平方メートル|m<sup>2</sup>]]）ある<ref name="uy">宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：13ページ</ref>。地域では「雨の宮」または「雨の宮さん」と呼び、日照りが続くと[[雨乞い]]祈願が行われていた<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk">学研パブリッシング（2013）：69ページ</ref><ref name="ik">{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course18.html |title=コース18 玉城ルート1 〜里山の神社を巡る〜 | publisher=伊勢市観光協会 |language=日本語 |accessdate=2014-02-27|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20131026234812/http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course18.html |archivedate=2013-10-26}}</ref>。
[[祭神]]は、高水上命（たかみなかみのみこと）<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>。水の神であり、玉城町内にある内宮摂社の[[坂手国生神社]]にも祀られている<ref name="th">金子（1984）：59ページ</ref>。[[神体]]は[[石]]である<ref name="th"/>。
== 歴史 == 倭姫命が定めた神社である<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>。伊勢神宮の末社の定義により、『延暦儀式帳』成立、すなわち[[延暦]]23年（[[804年]]）以前に創建された<ref>伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：22ページ</ref>。内宮の御料田として地域を開拓した[[荒木田氏]]の一門は小社曽根に本拠を置き、小社神社を[[氏神]]として尊んできた<ref name="ic2">伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：58, 60ページ</ref><ref name="tm">辰巳出版（2013）：48ページ</ref>。『[[建久]]三年皇太神宮年中行事』（[[1192年]]）には[[4月 (旧暦)|旧暦4月]]の初申の日に一族で参拝したと記されている<ref name="tm"/>。
[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]になると[[武家]]勢力が侵入し、荒木田氏は内宮の鎮座する[[宇治 (伊勢市)|宇治]]へ移り、小社神社は衰退した<ref name="ic2"/>。神宮では明治の初期、社殿が中絶した摂末社21社の再興を目指した<ref name="st1">櫻井（1991）：281ページ</ref>。まず[[1874年]]（明治7年）、[[宇治山田神社]]・[[鴨下神社]]・[[津布良神社]]・[[大津神社 (伊勢市)|大津神社]]の再興が実現した<ref>櫻井（1991）：280 - 281ページ</ref>。続いて[[1875年]]（明治8年）には5社の再興を教部大輔に願い出た<ref name="st1"/>。この願い出は聞き届けられなかったが、[[1880年]]（明治13年）に[[御塩殿神社]]の東西御倉の古材をもって小社神社と[[葭原神社]]の社殿が造営された<ref name="st1"/>。残る15社については再興されることはなかった<ref name="st1"/>。
[[1911年]]（明治44年）3月に造り替えられた<ref name="uy"/>。
== 交通 == 小社神社は周囲を[[田]]に囲まれている<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>。[[三重県道717号岩出田丸線]]から少し入ったところにある。 ;公共交通 * [[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[参宮線]][[田丸駅]]より南東へ徒歩約25分<ref name="tm"/>（約2.0[[キロメートル|km]]<!--Yahoo!ロコによるルート検索結果より-->）。 ;自家用車 :[[伊勢自動車道]][[玉城インターチェンジ|玉城IC]]より約10分（約5.0[[キロメートル|km]]）<!--Yahoo!ロコによるルート検索結果より-->。付近に[[駐車場]]はない。

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[小社神社]] to [[Ogosō Shrine]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 小社神社 |画像 = [[File:Ogoso-jinja (Naiku) 01.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[玉城町]]小社曽根字小社山1955 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 46.8 | 経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 38 | 経度秒 = 52.8 |祭神 = 高水上命 |社格 = [[皇大神宮]]末社 |創建 = [[延暦]]23年（[[804年]]）以前 |本殿 = [[神明造]] |別名 = 雨の宮さん |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = [[神嘗祭]] }} '''小社神社'''（おごそじんじゃ）は、[[伊勢神宮]][[皇大神宮]]（内宮）の[[摂末社|末社]]。[[水神|水の神]]を祀る神社で、内宮の[[禰宜]]を務め鎮座地周辺を開拓した[[荒木田氏]]が本拠を置いていた地にある<ref name="ic">伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：58ページ</ref>。
== 概要 == [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[玉城町]]小社曽根（おごそそね）に鎮座する<ref name="ic"/>。玉城町にある13の内宮摂末社のうちの1社である。内宮の末社16社のうち第4位である<ref>宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：13 - 14ページ</ref>。社地の面積は7反22歩（≒7,014.9[[平方メートル|m<sup>2</sup>]]）ある<ref name="uy">宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：13ページ</ref>。地域では「雨の宮」または「雨の宮さん」と呼び、日照りが続くと[[雨乞い]]祈願が行われていた<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk">学研パブリッシング（2013）：69ページ</ref><ref name="ik">{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course18.html |title=コース18 玉城ルート1 〜里山の神社を巡る〜 | publisher=伊勢市観光協会 |language=日本語 |accessdate=2014-02-27|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20131026234812/http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course18.html |archivedate=2013-10-26}}</ref>。
[[祭神]]は、高水上命（たかみなかみのみこと）<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>。水の神であり、玉城町内にある内宮摂社の[[坂手国生神社]]にも祀られている<ref name="th">金子（1984）：59ページ</ref>。[[神体]]は[[石]]である<ref name="th"/>。
== 歴史 == 倭姫命が定めた神社である<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>。伊勢神宮の末社の定義により、『延暦儀式帳』成立、すなわち[[延暦]]23年（[[804年]]）以前に創建された<ref>伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：22ページ</ref>。内宮の御料田として地域を開拓した[[荒木田氏]]の一門は小社曽根に本拠を置き、小社神社を[[氏神]]として尊んできた<ref name="ic2">伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：58, 60ページ</ref><ref name="tm">辰巳出版（2013）：48ページ</ref>。『[[建久]]三年皇太神宮年中行事』（[[1192年]]）には[[4月 (旧暦)|旧暦4月]]の初申の日に一族で参拝したと記されている<ref name="tm"/>。
[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]になると[[武家]]勢力が侵入し、荒木田氏は内宮の鎮座する[[宇治 (伊勢市)|宇治]]へ移り、小社神社は衰退した<ref name="ic2"/>。神宮では明治の初期、社殿が中絶した摂末社21社の再興を目指した<ref name="st1">櫻井（1991）：281ページ</ref>。まず[[1874年]]（明治7年）、[[宇治山田神社]]・[[鴨下神社]]・[[津布良神社]]・[[大津神社 (伊勢市)|大津神社]]の再興が実現した<ref>櫻井（1991）：280 - 281ページ</ref>。続いて[[1875年]]（明治8年）には5社の再興を教部大輔に願い出た<ref name="st1"/>。この願い出は聞き届けられなかったが、[[1880年]]（明治13年）に[[御塩殿神社]]の東西御倉の古材をもって小社神社と[[葭原神社]]の社殿が造営された<ref name="st1"/>。残る15社については再興されることはなかった<ref name="st1"/>。
[[1911年]]（明治44年）3月に造り替えられた<ref name="uy"/>。
== 交通 == 小社神社は周囲を[[田]]に囲まれている<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>。[[三重県道717号岩出田丸線]]から少し入ったところにある。 ;公共交通 * [[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[参宮線]][[田丸駅]]より南東へ徒歩約25分<ref name="tm"/>（約2.0[[キロメートル|km]]<!--Yahoo!ロコによるルート検索結果より-->）。 ;自家用車 :[[伊勢自動車道]][[玉城インターチェンジ|玉城IC]]より約10分（約5.0[[キロメートル|km]]）<!--Yahoo!ロコによるルート検索結果より-->。付近に[[駐車場]]はない。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}

ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013年11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7
