{{神社 |名称 = 小社神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Ogoso-jinja (Naiku) 01.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[玉城町]]小社曽根字小社山1955 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 46.8 | 経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 38 | 経度秒 = 52.8 |祭神 = 高水上命 |社格 = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}末社 |創建 = [[延暦]]23rd year （[[804]]）以前 |本殿 = [[神明造]] |別名 = 雨の宮さん |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Ogosō Shrine''' (小社神社) is a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|摂末社|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (Inner Shrine) within the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} complex. This shrine enshrines a {{ill|suijin|ja|水神|1=Suijin|lt=water god|lt=water deity|12=draft|12=draft}}, and it is located in an area that was historically the base of the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, who served as {{ill|negi (Shinto)|ja|禰宜|12=draft|12=draft}} (senior priests) at the Inner Shrine and developed the surrounding region.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing, ed. (2008), p.58.</ref>
== Overview ==
Ogoso Shrine (小社神社) is located in Ogoso Sone, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture. It is one of the 13 auxiliary and subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine (Naiku) of Ise Grand Shrine. Among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine, it Ranks 4th. The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 7,014.9 square meters. Locally, it is affectionately known as "Ame no Miya" or "Ame no Miya-san" and has been a place for rain-making prayers during droughts.
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is Takaminakami no Mikoto (高水上命), a water deity also worshipped at {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社|12=draft|12=draft}} within Tamaki Town. The sacred object of worship is a stone.
== History ==
Ogoso Shrine was established by Yamatohime-no-Mikoto. According to the definition of subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, it was founded before the Completion tier 4]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. {{translated page|ja|小社神社|version=98019726|comment=Imported full ja history}}
, {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|12=draft|12=draft}} was accomplished. In 1875, a petition was submitted to the Ministry of Religion for the restoration of five more shrines, but it was not granted. However, in 1880, Ogoso Shrine and {{ill|Ashihara Shrine|ja|葭原神社|12=draft|12=draft}} were reconstructed using old materials from the East and West storehouses of {{ill|Mishioden Shrine|ja|御塩殿神社|12=draft|12=draft}}. The remaining 15 shrines were never restored.
The shrine was reconstructed in March 1911.
== Access ==
Ogoso Shrine is surrounded by rice fields and is located slightly off Mie Prefectural Route 717 Iwadetamura Line.
=== Public Transport === * Approximately a 25-minute walk southeast (about 2.0 km) from Tamura Station on the JR Sangu Line.
=== By Car === * Approximately 10 minutes (about 5.0 km) from Tamaki IC on the Ise Expressway. There is no parking available nearby. == References ==

== 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]]
}}
{{draft categories| [[Category:Branch shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category: of the "Enryaku Gishikicho" in 804. The Arakida clan, who developed the area as the sacred rice fields of the Inner Shrine, were based in Ogoso Sone and revered Ogoso Shrine as their clan deity. The "Kenkyu Third Year Annual Events of the Imperial Grand Shrine" (1192) records that the clan visited the shrine on the first Monkey day of the 4th lunar month.
During the Sengoku period, the Arakida clan relocated to Uji, where the Inner Shrine is located, due to the incursion of warrior factions, leading to the decline of Ogoso Shrine. In the early Meiji period, the Grand Shrine of Ise aimed to revive 21 auxiliary and subordinate shrines whose shrines had ceased to exist. In 1874, the restoration of {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社|12=draft|12=draft}}
[[ja:小社神社]] [[sn:Ogosō Shrine]] [[en:小社神社]]

=== By Car === * Approximately 10 minutes (about 5.0 km) from Tamaki IC on the Ise Expressway. There is no parking available nearby. == References ==

{{draft categories| [[Category:Branch shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category: of the "Enryaku Gishikicho" in 804. The Arakida clan, who developed the area as the sacred rice fields of the Inner Shrine, were based in Ogoso Sone and revered Ogoso Shrine as their clan deity. The "Kenkyu Third Year Annual Events of the Imperial Grand Shrine" (1192) records that the clan visited the shrine on the first Monkey day of the 4th lunar month.
During the Sengoku period, the Arakida clan relocated to Uji, where the Inner Shrine is located, due to the incursion of warrior factions, leading to the decline of Ogoso Shrine. In the early Meiji period, the Grand Shrine of Ise aimed to revive 21 auxiliary and subordinate shrines whose shrines had ceased to exist. In 1874, the restoration of {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
[[ja:小社神社]] [[sn:Ogosō Shrine]] [[en:小社神社]]

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 小社神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Ogoso-jinja (Naiku) 01.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[玉城町]]小社曽根字小社山1955 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 46.8 | 経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 38 | 経度秒 = 52.8 |祭神 = 高水上命 |社格 = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}末社 |創建 = [[延暦]]23rd year （[[804]]）以前 |本殿 = [[神明造]] |別名 = 雨の宮さん |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Ogosō Shrine''' (小社神社) is a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|摂末社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Inner Shrine) within the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} complex. This shrine enshrines a {{ill|suijin|ja|水神|1=Suijin|lt=water god|lt=water deity|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and it is located in an area that was historically the base of the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who served as {{ill|negi (Shinto)|ja|禰宜|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (senior priests) at the Inner Shrine and developed the surrounding region.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing, ed. (2008), p.58.</ref>
== Overview ==
Ogoso Shrine (小社神社) is located in Ogoso Sone, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture. It is one of the 13 auxiliary and subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine (Naiku) of Ise Grand Shrine. Among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine, it Ranks 4th. The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 7,014.9 square meters. Locally, it is affectionately known as "Ame no Miya" or "Ame no Miya-san" and has been a place for rain-making prayers during droughts.
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is Takaminakami no Mikoto (高水上命), a water deity also worshipped at {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} within Tamaki Town. The sacred object of worship is a stone.
== History ==
Ogoso Shrine was established by Yamatohime-no-Mikoto. According to the definition of subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, it was founded before the Completion tier 4]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第3巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第92集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和59th year 3月10日、308p. * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [[下外城田村]]
== External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area04/area04_07.jpg 小社神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m08/0804.html 小社神社（おごそじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ 産業観光神社めぐり] - 三重県玉城町 {{125 Shrines of Ise}}
{{translated page|ja|小社神社|version=98019726|comment=Imported full ja history}}
, {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was accomplished. In 1875, a petition was submitted to the Ministry of Religion for the restoration of five more shrines, but it was not granted. However, in 1880, Ogoso Shrine and {{ill|Ashihara Shrine|ja|葭原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} were reconstructed using old materials from the East and West storehouses of {{ill|Mishioden Shrine|ja|御塩殿神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The remaining 15 shrines were never restored.
The shrine was reconstructed in March 1911.
== Access ==
Ogoso Shrine is surrounded by rice fields and is located slightly off Mie Prefectural Route 717 Iwadetamura Line.
=== Public Transport === * Approximately a 25-minute walk southeast (about 2.0 km) from Tamura Station on the JR Sangu Line.
=== By Car === * Approximately 10 minutes (about 5.0 km) from Tamaki IC on the Ise Expressway. There is no parking available nearby. == References ==

{{神社 |名称 = 小社神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Ogoso-jinja (Naiku) 01.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[玉城町]]小社曽根字小社山1955 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 46.8 | 経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 38 | 経度秒 = 52.8 |祭神 = 高水上命 |社格 = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}}末社 |創建 = [[延暦]]23rd year （[[804]]）以前 |本殿 = [[神明造]] |別名 = 雨の宮さん |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Ogosō Shrine''' (小社神社) is a [[:en:Setsumatsusha|branch shrine]] of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} (Inner Shrine) within the [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]] complex. This shrine enshrines a [[:en:Suijin|water deity]], and it is located in an area that was historically the base of the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakida clan|qq=}}, who served as {{ill|negi (Shinto)|ja|禰宜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/negi (Shinto)|qq=}} (senior priests) at the Inner Shrine and developed the surrounding region.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing, ed. (2008), p.58.</ref>
== Overview ==
Ogoso Shrine (小社神社) is located in Ogoso Sone, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture. It is one of the 13 auxiliary and subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine (Naiku) of Ise Grand Shrine. Among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine, it Ranks 4th. The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 7,014.9 square meters. Locally, it is affectionately known as "Ame no Miya" or "Ame no Miya-san" and has been a place for rain-making prayers during droughts.
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is Takaminakami no Mikoto (高水上命), a water deity also worshipped at {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakatekunari Shrine|qq=}} within Tamaki Town. The sacred object of worship is a stone.
== History ==
Ogoso Shrine was established by Yamatohime-no-Mikoto. According to the definition of subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, it was founded before the Completion tier 4]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第3巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第92集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和59th year 3月10日、308p. * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[下外城田村]]
== External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area04/area04_07.jpg 小社神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m08/0804.html 小社神社（おごそじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ 産業観光神社めぐり] - 三重県玉城町 {{125 Shrines of Ise}}
{{translated page|ja|小社神社|version=98019726|comment=Imported full ja history}}
, {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamoshimo Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubura Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} was accomplished. In 1875, a petition was submitted to the Ministry of Religion for the restoration of five more shrines, but it was not granted. However, in 1880, Ogoso Shrine and {{ill|Ashihara Shrine|ja|葭原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihara Shrine|qq=}} were reconstructed using old materials from the East and West storehouses of {{ill|Mishioden Shrine|ja|御塩殿神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishioden Shrine|qq=}}. The remaining 15 shrines were never restored.
The shrine was reconstructed in March 1911.
== Access ==
Ogoso Shrine is surrounded by rice fields and is located slightly off Mie Prefectural Route 717 Iwadetamura Line.
=== Public Transport === * Approximately a 25-minute walk southeast (about 2.0 km) from Tamura Station on the JR Sangu Line.
=== By Car === * Approximately 10 minutes (about 5.0 km) from Tamaki IC on the Ise Expressway. There is no parking available nearby. == References ==
{{Reflist|2}}
== 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]]
}}
{{draft categories| [[Category:Branch shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category: of the "Enryaku Gishikicho" in 804. The Arakida clan, who developed the area as the sacred rice fields of the Inner Shrine, were based in Ogoso Sone and revered Ogoso Shrine as their clan deity. The "Kenkyu Third Year Annual Events of the Imperial Grand Shrine" (1192) records that the clan visited the shrine on the first Monkey day of the 4th lunar month.
During the Sengoku period, the Arakida clan relocated to Uji, where the Inner Shrine is located, due to the incursion of warrior factions, leading to the decline of Ogoso Shrine. In the early Meiji period, the Grand Shrine of Ise aimed to revive 21 auxiliary and subordinate shrines whose shrines had ceased to exist. In 1874, the restoration of {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyoda Shrine|qq=}}
[[ja:小社神社]] [[sn:Ogosō Shrine]] [[en:小社神社]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]]

{{神社 |名称 = 小社神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Ogoso-jinja (Naiku) 01.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[玉城町]]小社曽根字小社山1955 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 46.8 | 経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 38 | 経度秒 = 52.8 |祭神 = 高水上命 |社格 = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}}末社 |創建 = [[延暦]]23rd year （[[804]]）以前 |本殿 = [[神明造]] |別名 = 雨の宮さん |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Ogosō Shrine''' (小社神社) is a [[:en:Setsumatsusha|branch shrine]] of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} (Inner Shrine) within the [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]] complex. This shrine enshrines a [[:en:Suijin|water deity]], and it is located in an area that was historically the base of the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakida clan|qq=}}, who served as {{ill|negi (Shinto)|ja|禰宜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/negi (Shinto)|qq=}} (senior priests) at the Inner Shrine and developed the surrounding region.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing, ed. (2008), p.58.</ref>
== Overview ==
Ogoso Shrine (小社神社) is located in Ogoso Sone, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture. It is one of the 13 auxiliary and subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine (Naiku) of Ise Grand Shrine. Among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine, it Ranks 4th. The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 7,014.9 square meters. Locally, it is affectionately known as "Ame no Miya" or "Ame no Miya-san" and has been a place for rain-making prayers during droughts.
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is Takaminakami no Mikoto (高水上命), a water deity also worshipped at {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakatekunari Shrine|qq=}} within Tamaki Town. The sacred object of worship is a stone.
== History ==
Ogoso Shrine was established by Yamatohime-no-Mikoto. According to the definition of subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, it was founded before the Completion tier 4]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第3巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第92集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和59th year 3月10日、308p. * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[下外城田村]]
== External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area04/area04_07.jpg 小社神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m08/0804.html 小社神社（おごそじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ 産業観光神社めぐり] - 三重県玉城町 {{125 Shrines of Ise}}
{{translated page|ja|小社神社|version=98019726|comment=Imported full ja history}}
, {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamoshimo Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubura Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} was accomplished. In 1875, a petition was submitted to the Ministry of Religion for the restoration of five more shrines, but it was not granted. However, in 1880, Ogoso Shrine and {{ill|Ashihara Shrine|ja|葭原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihara Shrine|qq=}} were reconstructed using old materials from the East and West storehouses of {{ill|Mishioden Shrine|ja|御塩殿神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishioden Shrine|qq=}}. The remaining 15 shrines were never restored.
The shrine was reconstructed in March 1911.
== Access ==
Ogoso Shrine is surrounded by rice fields and is located slightly off Mie Prefectural Route 717 Iwadetamura Line.
=== Public Transport === * Approximately a 25-minute walk southeast (about 2.0 km) from Tamura Station on the JR Sangu Line.
=== By Car === * Approximately 10 minutes (about 5.0 km) from Tamaki IC on the Ise Expressway. There is no parking available nearby. == References ==
{{Reflist|2}}
== 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]]
}}
{{draft categories| [[Category:Branch shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category: of the "Enryaku Gishikicho" in 804. The Arakida clan, who developed the area as the sacred rice fields of the Inner Shrine, were based in Ogoso Sone and revered Ogoso Shrine as their clan deity. The "Kenkyu Third Year Annual Events of the Imperial Grand Shrine" (1192) records that the clan visited the shrine on the first Monkey day of the 4th lunar month.
During the Sengoku period, the Arakida clan relocated to Uji, where the Inner Shrine is located, due to the incursion of warrior factions, leading to the decline of Ogoso Shrine. In the early Meiji period, the Grand Shrine of Ise aimed to revive 21 auxiliary and subordinate shrines whose shrines had ceased to exist. In 1874, the restoration of {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyoda Shrine|qq=}}
[[ja:小社神社]] [[sn:Ogosō Shrine]] [[en:小社神社]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]]

{{神社 |名称 = 小社神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Ogoso-jinja (Naiku) 01.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[玉城町]]小社曽根字小社山1955 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 46.8 | 経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 38 | 経度秒 = 52.8 |祭神 = 高水上命 |社格 = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}末社 |創建 = [[延暦]]23rd year （[[804]]）以前 |本殿 = [[神明造]] |別名 = 雨の宮さん |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Ogosō Shrine''' (小社神社) is a [[:en:Setsumatsusha|branch shrine]] of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Inner Shrine) within the [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]] complex. This shrine enshrines a [[:en:Suijin|water deity]], and it is located in an area that was historically the base of the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakida clan|qq=}}, who served as {{ill|negi (Shinto)|ja|禰宜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/negi (Shinto)|qq=}} (senior priests) at the Inner Shrine and developed the surrounding region.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing, ed. (2008), p.58.</ref>
== Overview ==
Ogoso Shrine (小社神社) is located in Ogoso Sone, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture. It is one of the 13 auxiliary and subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine (Naiku) of Ise Grand Shrine. Among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine, it Ranks 4th. The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 7,014.9 square meters. Locally, it is affectionately known as "Ame no Miya" or "Ame no Miya-san" and has been a place for rain-making prayers during droughts.
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is Takaminakami no Mikoto (高水上命), a water deity also worshipped at {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakatekunari Shrine|qq=}} within Tamaki Town. The sacred object of worship is a stone.
== History ==
Ogoso Shrine was established by Yamatohime-no-Mikoto. According to the definition of subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, it was founded before the Completion tier 4]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第3巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第92集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和59th year 3月10日、308p. * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area04/area04_07.jpg 小社神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m08/0804.html 小社神社（おごそじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ 産業観光神社めぐり] - 三重県玉城町 {{125 Shrines of Ise}}
{{translated page|ja|小社神社|version=98019726|comment=Imported full ja history}}
, {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamoshimo Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubura Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} was accomplished. In 1875, a petition was submitted to the Ministry of Religion for the restoration of five more shrines, but it was not granted. However, in 1880, Ogoso Shrine and {{ill|Ashihara Shrine|ja|葭原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihara Shrine|qq=}} were reconstructed using old materials from the East and West storehouses of {{ill|Mishioden Shrine|ja|御塩殿神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishioden Shrine|qq=}}. The remaining 15 shrines were never restored.
The shrine was reconstructed in March 1911.
== Access ==
Ogoso Shrine is surrounded by rice fields and is located slightly off Mie Prefectural Route 717 Iwadetamura Line.
=== Public Transport === * Approximately a 25-minute walk southeast (about 2.0 km) from Tamura Station on the JR Sangu Line.
=== By Car === * Approximately 10 minutes (about 5.0 km) from Tamaki IC on the Ise Expressway. There is no parking available nearby. == References ==

{{draft categories| [[Category:Branch shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category: of the "Enryaku Gishikicho" in 804. The Arakida clan, who developed the area as the sacred rice fields of the Inner Shrine, were based in Ogoso Sone and revered Ogoso Shrine as their clan deity. The "Kenkyu Third Year Annual Events of the Imperial Grand Shrine" (1192) records that the clan visited the shrine on the first Monkey day of the 4th lunar month.
During the Sengoku period, the Arakida clan relocated to Uji, where the Inner Shrine is located, due to the incursion of warrior factions, leading to the decline of Ogoso Shrine. In the early Meiji period, the Grand Shrine of Ise aimed to revive 21 auxiliary and subordinate shrines whose shrines had ceased to exist. In 1874, the restoration of {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyoda Shrine|qq=}}

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 小社神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Ogoso-jinja (Naiku) 01.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[玉城町]]小社曽根字小社山1955 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 46.8 | 経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 38 | 経度秒 = 52.8 |祭神 = 高水上命 |社格 = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}末社 |創建 = [[延暦]]23rd year （[[804]]）以前 |本殿 = [[神明造]] |別名 = 雨の宮さん |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Ogosō Shrine''' (小社神社) is a [[:en:Setsumatsusha|branch shrine]] of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Inner Shrine) within the [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]] complex. This shrine enshrines a [[:en:Suijin|water deity]], and it is located in an area that was historically the base of the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakida clan|qq=}}, who served as {{ill|negi (Shinto)|ja|禰宜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/negi (Shinto)|qq=}} (senior priests) at the Inner Shrine and developed the surrounding region.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing, ed. (2008), p.58.</ref>
== Overview ==
Ogoso Shrine (小社神社) is located in Ogoso Sone, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture. It is one of the 13 auxiliary and subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine (Naiku) of Ise Grand Shrine. Among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine, it Ranks 4th. The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 7,014.9 square meters. Locally, it is affectionately known as "Ame no Miya" or "Ame no Miya-san" and has been a place for rain-making prayers during droughts.
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is Takaminakami no Mikoto (高水上命), a water deity also worshipped at {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakatekunari Shrine|qq=}} within Tamaki Town. The sacred object of worship is a stone.
== History ==
Ogoso Shrine was established by Yamatohime-no-Mikoto. According to the definition of subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, it was founded before the Completion tier 4]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第3巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第92集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和59th year 3月10日、308p. * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[下外城田村]]
== External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area04/area04_07.jpg 小社神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m08/0804.html 小社神社（おごそじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ 産業観光神社めぐり] - 三重県玉城町 {{125 Shrines of Ise}}
{{translated page|ja|小社神社|version=98019726|comment=Imported full ja history}}
, {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamoshimo Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubura Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} was accomplished. In 1875, a petition was submitted to the Ministry of Religion for the restoration of five more shrines, but it was not granted. However, in 1880, Ogoso Shrine and {{ill|Ashihara Shrine|ja|葭原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihara Shrine|qq=}} were reconstructed using old materials from the East and West storehouses of {{ill|Mishioden Shrine|ja|御塩殿神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishioden Shrine|qq=}}. The remaining 15 shrines were never restored.
The shrine was reconstructed in March 1911.
== Access ==
Ogoso Shrine is surrounded by rice fields and is located slightly off Mie Prefectural Route 717 Iwadetamura Line.
=== Public Transport === * Approximately a 25-minute walk southeast (about 2.0 km) from Tamura Station on the JR Sangu Line.
=== By Car === * Approximately 10 minutes (about 5.0 km) from Tamaki IC on the Ise Expressway. There is no parking available nearby. == References ==
{{Reflist|2}}
== 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]]
}}
{{draft categories|
[[Category: of the "Enryaku Gishikicho" in 804. The Arakida clan, who developed the area as the sacred rice fields of the Inner Shrine, were based in Ogoso Sone and revered Ogoso Shrine as their clan deity. The "Kenkyu Third Year Annual Events of the Imperial Grand Shrine" (1192) records that the clan visited the shrine on the first Monkey day of the 4th lunar month.
During the Sengoku period, the Arakida clan relocated to Uji, where the Inner Shrine is located, due to the incursion of warrior factions, leading to the decline of Ogoso Shrine. In the early Meiji period, the Grand Shrine of Ise aimed to revive 21 auxiliary and subordinate shrines whose shrines had ceased to exist. In 1874, the restoration of {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyoda Shrine|qq=}}
[[ja:小社神社]] [[sn:Ogosō Shrine]] [[en:小社神社]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis-->

revision.comment: Merge [[Category:玉城の Municipal History]] into [[Category:Municipal History of Tamaki]] via bot
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 小社神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Ogoso-jinja (Naiku) 01.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[玉城町]]小社曽根字小社山1955 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 46.8 | 経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 38 | 経度秒 = 52.8 |祭神 = 高水上命 |社格 = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}末社 |創建 = [[延暦]]23rd year （[[804]]）以前 |本殿 = [[神明造]] |別名 = 雨の宮さん |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Ogosō Shrine''' (小社神社) is a [[:en:Setsumatsusha|branch shrine]] of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Inner Shrine) within the [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]] complex. This shrine enshrines a [[:en:Suijin|water deity]], and it is located in an area that was historically the base of the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakida clan|qq=}}, who served as {{ill|negi (Shinto)|ja|禰宜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/negi (Shinto)|qq=}} (senior priests) at the Inner Shrine and developed the surrounding region.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing, ed. (2008), p.58.</ref>
== Overview ==
Ogoso Shrine (小社神社) is located in Ogoso Sone, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture. It is one of the 13 auxiliary and subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine (Naiku) of Ise Grand Shrine. Among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine, it Ranks 4th. The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 7,014.9 square meters. Locally, it is affectionately known as "Ame no Miya" or "Ame no Miya-san" and has been a place for rain-making prayers during droughts.
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is Takaminakami no Mikoto (高水上命), a water deity also worshipped at {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakatekunari Shrine|qq=}} within Tamaki Town. The sacred object of worship is a stone.
== History ==
Ogoso Shrine was established by Yamatohime-no-Mikoto. According to the definition of subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, it was founded before the Completion tier 4]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第3巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第92集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和59th year 3月10日、308p. * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[下外城田村]]
== External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area04/area04_07.jpg 小社神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m08/0804.html 小社神社（おごそじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ 産業観光神社めぐり] - 三重県玉城町 {{125 Shrines of Ise}}
{{translated page|ja|小社神社|version=98019726|comment=Imported full ja history}}
, {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamoshimo Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubura Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} was accomplished. In 1875, a petition was submitted to the Ministry of Religion for the restoration of five more shrines, but it was not granted. However, in 1880, Ogoso Shrine and {{ill|Ashihara Shrine|ja|葭原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihara Shrine|qq=}} were reconstructed using old materials from the East and West storehouses of {{ill|Mishioden Shrine|ja|御塩殿神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishioden Shrine|qq=}}. The remaining 15 shrines were never restored.
The shrine was reconstructed in March 1911.
== Access ==
Ogoso Shrine is surrounded by rice fields and is located slightly off Mie Prefectural Route 717 Iwadetamura Line.
=== Public Transport === * Approximately a 25-minute walk southeast (about 2.0 km) from Tamura Station on the JR Sangu Line.
=== By Car === * Approximately 10 minutes (about 5.0 km) from Tamaki IC on the Ise Expressway. There is no parking available nearby. == References ==
{{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]]
}}
{{draft categories|
[[Category: of the "Enryaku Gishikicho" in 804. The Arakida clan, who developed the area as the sacred rice fields of the Inner Shrine, were based in Ogoso Sone and revered Ogoso Shrine as their clan deity. The "Kenkyu Third Year Annual Events of the Imperial Grand Shrine" (1192) records that the clan visited the shrine on the first Monkey day of the 4th lunar month.
During the Sengoku period, the Arakida clan relocated to Uji, where the Inner Shrine is located, due to the incursion of warrior factions, leading to the decline of Ogoso Shrine. In the early Meiji period, the Grand Shrine of Ise aimed to revive 21 auxiliary and subordinate shrines whose shrines had ceased to exist. In 1874, the restoration of {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyoda Shrine|qq=}}
[[ja:小社神社]] [[sn:Ogosō Shrine]] [[en:小社神社]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:Municipal History of Tamaki]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis-->

In 1874, the restoration of {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyoda Shrine|qq=}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17224784]] Wikidata name: Ogoso-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |名称 = 小社神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Ogoso-jinja (Naiku) 01.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[玉城町]]小社曽根字小社山1955 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 46.8 | 経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 38 | 経度秒 = 52.8 |祭神 = 高水上命 |社格 = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}末社 |創建 = [[延暦]]23rd year （[[804]]）以前 |本殿 = [[神明造]] |別名 = 雨の宮さん |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Ogosō Shrine''' (小社神社) is a [[:en:Setsumatsusha|branch shrine]] of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Inner Shrine) within the [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]] complex. This shrine enshrines a [[:en:Suijin|water deity]], and it is located in an area that was historically the base of the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakida clan|qq=}}, who served as {{ill|negi (Shinto)|ja|禰宜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/negi (Shinto)|qq=}} (senior priests) at the Inner Shrine and developed the surrounding region.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing, ed. (2008), p.58.</ref>
== Overview ==
Ogoso Shrine (小社神社) is located in Ogoso Sone, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture. It is one of the 13 auxiliary and subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine (Naiku) of Ise Grand Shrine. Among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine, it Ranks 4th. The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 7,014.9 square meters. Locally, it is affectionately known as "Ame no Miya" or "Ame no Miya-san" and has been a place for rain-making prayers during droughts.
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is Takaminakami no Mikoto (高水上命), a water deity also worshipped at {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakatekunari Shrine|qq=}} within Tamaki Town. The sacred object of worship is a stone.
== History ==
Ogoso Shrine was established by Yamatohime-no-Mikoto. According to the definition of subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, it was founded before the Completion tier 4]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第3巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第92集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和59th year 3月10日、308p. * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[下外城田村]]
== External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area04/area04_07.jpg 小社神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m08/0804.html 小社神社（おごそじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ 産業観光神社めぐり] - 三重県玉城町 {{125 Shrines of Ise}}
{{translated page|ja|小社神社|version=98019726|comment=Imported full ja history}}
, {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamoshimo Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubura Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} was accomplished. In 1875, a petition was submitted to the Ministry of Religion for the restoration of five more shrines, but it was not granted. However, in 1880, Ogoso Shrine and {{ill|Ashihara Shrine|ja|葭原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihara Shrine|qq=}} were reconstructed using old materials from the East and West storehouses of {{ill|Mishioden Shrine|ja|御塩殿神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishioden Shrine|qq=}}. The remaining 15 shrines were never restored.
The shrine was reconstructed in March 1911.
== Access ==
Ogoso Shrine is surrounded by rice fields and is located slightly off Mie Prefectural Route 717 Iwadetamura Line.
=== Public Transport === * Approximately a 25-minute walk southeast (about 2.0 km) from Tamura Station on the JR Sangu Line.
=== By Car === * Approximately 10 minutes (about 5.0 km) from Tamaki IC on the Ise Expressway. There is no parking available nearby. == References ==

[[Category: of the "Enryaku Gishikicho" in 804. The Arakida clan, who developed the area as the sacred rice fields of the Inner Shrine, were based in Ogoso Sone and revered Ogoso Shrine as their clan deity. The "Kenkyu Third Year Annual Events of the Imperial Grand Shrine" (1192) records that the clan visited the shrine on the first Monkey day of the 4th lunar month.
During the Sengoku period, the Arakida clan relocated to Uji, where the Inner Shrine is located, due to the incursion of warrior factions, leading to the decline of Ogoso Shrine. In the early Meiji period, the Grand Shrine of Ise aimed to revive 21 auxiliary and subordinate shrines whose shrines had ceased to exist. In 1874, the restoration of {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyoda Shrine|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:小社神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17224784]] Wikidata name: Ogoso-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |名称 = 小社神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Ogoso-jinja (Naiku) 01.jpg |所在地 = [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[玉城町]]小社曽根字小社山1955 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 46.8 | 経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 38 | 経度秒 = 52.8 |祭神 = 高水上命 |社格 = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}末社 |創建 = [[延暦]]23rd year （[[804]]）以前 |本殿 = [[神明造]] |別名 = 雨の宮さん |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = [[神嘗祭]] |prefecture= {{ill|Tamaki|qid=Q587348}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.479667|N|136.648|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Ogosō Shrine''' (小社神社) is a [[:en:Setsumatsusha|branch shrine]] of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Inner Shrine) within the [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]] complex. This shrine enshrines a [[:en:Suijin|water deity]], and it is located in an area that was historically the base of the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakida clan|qq=}}, who served as {{ill|negi (Shinto)|ja|禰宜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/negi (Shinto)|qq=|1=Negi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594490}} (senior priests) at the Inner Shrine and developed the surrounding region.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing, ed. (2008), p.58.</ref>
== Overview ==
Ogoso Shrine (小社神社) is located in Ogoso Sone, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture. It is one of the 13 auxiliary and subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine (Naiku) of Ise Grand Shrine. Among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine, it Ranks 4th. The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 7,014.9 square meters. Locally, it is affectionately known as "Ame no Miya" or "Ame no Miya-san" and has been a place for rain-making prayers during droughts.
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is Takaminakami no Mikoto (高水上命), a water deity also worshipped at {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakatekunari Shrine|qq=}} within Tamaki Town. The sacred object of worship is a stone.
== History ==
Ogoso Shrine was established by Yamatohime-no-Mikoto. According to the definition of subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, it was founded before the Completion tier 4]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第3巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第92集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和59th year 3月10日、308p. * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[下外城田村]]
== External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area04/area04_07.jpg 小社神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m08/0804.html 小社神社（おごそじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ 産業観光神社めぐり] - 三重県玉城町 {{125 Shrines of Ise}}
{{translated page|ja|小社神社|version=98019726|comment=Imported full ja history}}
, {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamoshimo Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubura Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} was accomplished. In 1875, a petition was submitted to the Ministry of Religion for the restoration of five more shrines, but it was not granted. However, in 1880, Ogoso Shrine and {{ill|Ashihara Shrine|ja|葭原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihara Shrine|qq=}} were reconstructed using old materials from the East and West storehouses of {{ill|Mishioden Shrine|ja|御塩殿神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishioden Shrine|qq=}}. The remaining 15 shrines were never restored.
The shrine was reconstructed in March 1911.
== Access ==
Ogoso Shrine is surrounded by rice fields and is located slightly off Mie Prefectural Route 717 Iwadetamura Line.
=== Public Transport === * Approximately a 25-minute walk southeast (about 2.0 km) from Tamura Station on the JR Sangu Line.
=== By Car === * Approximately 10 minutes (about 5.0 km) from Tamaki IC on the Ise Expressway. There is no parking available nearby. == References ==
{{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]]
}}

However, in 1880, Ogoso Shrine and {{ill|Ashihara Shrine|ja|葭原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihara Shrine|qq=}} were reconstructed using old materials from the East and West storehouses of {{ill|Mishioden Shrine|ja|御塩殿神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishioden Shrine|qq=}}. The remaining 15 shrines were never restored.
The shrine was reconstructed in March 1911.
== Access ==
Ogoso Shrine is surrounded by rice fields and is located slightly off Mie Prefectural Route 717 Iwadetamura Line.
=== Public Transport === * Approximately a 25-minute walk southeast (about 2.0 km) from Tamura Station on the JR Sangu Line.
=== By Car === * Approximately 10 minutes (about 5.0 km) from Tamaki IC on the Ise Expressway. There is no parking available nearby. == References ==

[[Category: of the "Enryaku Gishikicho" in 804. The Arakida clan, who developed the area as the sacred rice fields of the Inner Shrine, were based in Ogoso Sone and revered Ogoso Shrine as their clan deity. The "Kenkyu Third Year Annual Events of the Imperial Grand Shrine" (1192) records that the clan visited the shrine on the first Monkey day of the 4th lunar month.
During the Sengoku period, the Arakida clan relocated to Uji, where the Inner Shrine is located, due to the incursion of warrior factions, leading to the decline of Ogoso Shrine. In the early Meiji period, the Grand Shrine of Ise aimed to revive 21 auxiliary and subordinate shrines whose shrines had ceased to exist. In 1874, the restoration of {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyoda Shrine|qq=}}

(2008), p.58.</ref>
== Overview ==
Ogoso Shrine (小社神社) is located in Ogoso Sone, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture. It is one of the 13 auxiliary and subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine (Naiku) of Ise Grand Shrine. Among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine, it Ranks 4th. The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 7,014.9 square meters. Locally, it is affectionately known as "Ame no Miya" or "Ame no Miya-san" and has been a place for rain-making prayers during droughts.
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is Takaminakami no Mikoto (高水上命), a water deity also worshipped at {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakatekunari Shrine|qq=}} within Tamaki Town. The sacred object of worship is a stone.
== History ==
Ogoso Shrine was established by Yamatohime-no-Mikoto. According to the definition of subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, it was founded before the Completion tier 4]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. {{translated page|ja|小社神社|version=98019726|comment=Imported full ja history}}
, {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamoshimo Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubura Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} was accomplished. In 1875, a petition was submitted to the Ministry of Religion for the restoration of five more shrines, but it was not granted. However, in 1880, Ogoso Shrine and {{ill|Ashihara Shrine|ja|葭原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihara Shrine|qq=}} were reconstructed using old materials from the East and West storehouses of {{ill|Mishioden Shrine|ja|御塩殿神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishioden Shrine|qq=}}. The remaining 15 shrines were never restored.
The shrine was reconstructed in March 1911.
== Access ==
Ogoso Shrine is surrounded by rice fields and is located slightly off Mie Prefectural Route 717 Iwadetamura Line.
=== Public Transport === * Approximately a 25-minute walk southeast (about 2.0 km) from Tamura Station on the JR Sangu Line.
=== By Car === * Approximately 10 minutes (about 5.0 km) from Tamaki IC on the Ise Expressway. There is no parking available nearby. == References ==

{{draft categories|
[[Category: of the "Enryaku Gishikicho" in 804. The Arakida clan, who developed the area as the sacred rice fields of the Inner Shrine, were based in Ogoso Sone and revered Ogoso Shrine as their clan deity. The "Kenkyu Third Year Annual Events of the Imperial Grand Shrine" (1192) records that the clan visited the shrine on the first Monkey day of the 4th lunar month.
During the Sengoku period, the Arakida clan relocated to Uji, where the Inner Shrine is located, due to the incursion of warrior factions, leading to the decline of Ogoso Shrine. In the early Meiji period, the Grand Shrine of Ise aimed to revive 21 auxiliary and subordinate shrines whose shrines had ceased to exist. In 1874, the restoration of {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyoda Shrine|qq=}}
[[ja:小社神社]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:小社神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17224784]] Wikidata name: Ogoso-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |名称 = 小社神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Ogoso-jinja (Naiku) 01.jpg |所在地 = [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[玉城町]]小社曽根字小社山1955 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 46.8 | 経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 38 | 経度秒 = 52.8 |祭神 = 高水上命 |社格 = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}末社 |創建 = [[延暦]]23rd year （[[804]]）以前 |本殿 = [[神明造]] |別名 = 雨の宮さん |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = [[神嘗祭]] |prefecture= {{ill|Tamaki|WD=Q587348}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.479667|N|136.648|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Ogosō Shrine''' (小社神社) is a [[:en:Setsumatsusha|branch shrine]] of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Inner Shrine) within the [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]] complex. This shrine enshrines a [[:en:Suijin|water deity]], and it is located in an area that was historically the base of the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakida clan|qq=}}, who served as {{ill|negi (Shinto)|ja|禰宜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/negi (Shinto)|1=Negi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594490}} (senior priests) at the Inner Shrine and developed the surrounding region.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing, ed. (2008), p.58.</ref>
== Overview ==
Ogoso Shrine (小社神社) is located in Ogoso Sone, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture. It is one of the 13 auxiliary and subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine (Naiku) of Ise Grand Shrine. Among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine, it Ranks 4th. The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 7,014.9 square meters. Locally, it is affectionately known as "Ame no Miya" or "Ame no Miya-san" and has been a place for rain-making prayers during droughts.
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is Takaminakami no Mikoto (高水上命), a water deity also worshipped at {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakatekunari Shrine|qq=}} within Tamaki Town. The sacred object of worship is a stone.
== History ==
Ogoso Shrine was established by Yamatohime-no-Mikoto. According to the definition of subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, it was founded before the Completion tier 4]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第3巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第92集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和59th year 3月10日、308p. * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[下外城田村]]

, {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamoshimo Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubura Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} was accomplished. In 1875, a petition was submitted to the Ministry of Religion for the restoration of five more shrines, but it was not granted. However, in 1880, Ogoso Shrine and {{ill|Ashihara Shrine|ja|葭原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihara Shrine|qq=}} were reconstructed using old materials from the East and West storehouses of {{ill|Mishioden Shrine|ja|御塩殿神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishioden Shrine|qq=}}. The remaining 15 shrines were never restored.
The shrine was reconstructed in March 1911.
== Access ==
Ogoso Shrine is surrounded by rice fields and is located slightly off Mie Prefectural Route 717 Iwadetamura Line.
=== Public Transport === * Approximately a 25-minute walk southeast (about 2.0 km) from Tamura Station on the JR Sangu Line.
=== By Car === * Approximately 10 minutes (about 5.0 km) from Tamaki IC on the Ise Expressway. There is no parking available nearby. == References ==

{{draft categories|
[[Category: of the "Enryaku Gishikicho" in 804. The Arakida clan, who developed the area as the sacred rice fields of the Inner Shrine, were based in Ogoso Sone and revered Ogoso Shrine as their clan deity. The "Kenkyu Third Year Annual Events of the Imperial Grand Shrine" (1192) records that the clan visited the shrine on the first Monkey day of the 4th lunar month.
During the Sengoku period, the Arakida clan relocated to Uji, where the Inner Shrine is located, due to the incursion of warrior factions, leading to the decline of Ogoso Shrine. In the early Meiji period, the Grand Shrine of Ise aimed to revive 21 auxiliary and subordinate shrines whose shrines had ceased to exist. In 1874, the restoration of {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyoda Shrine|qq=}}
[[ja:小社神社]]

(2008), p.58.</ref>
== Overview ==
Ogoso Shrine (小社神社) is located in Ogoso Sone, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture. It is one of the 13 auxiliary and subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine (Naiku) of Ise Grand Shrine. Among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine, it Ranks 4th. The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 7,014.9 square meters. Locally, it is affectionately known as "Ame no Miya" or "Ame no Miya-san" and has been a place for rain-making prayers during droughts.
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is Takaminakami no Mikoto (高水上命), a water deity also worshipped at {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakatekunari Shrine|qq=}} within Tamaki Town. The sacred object of worship is a stone.
== History ==
Ogoso Shrine was established by Yamatohime-no-Mikoto. According to the definition of subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, it was founded before the Completion tier 4]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. According to the definition of subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, it was founded before the Completion tier 4]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. {{translated page|ja|小社神社|version=98019726|comment=Imported full ja history}}
, {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamoshimo Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubura Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} was accomplished. In 1875, a petition was submitted to the Ministry of Religion for the restoration of five more shrines, but it was not granted. However, in 1880, Ogoso Shrine and {{ill|Ashihara Shrine|ja|葭原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihara Shrine|qq=}} were reconstructed using old materials from the East and West storehouses of {{ill|Mishioden Shrine|ja|御塩殿神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishioden Shrine|qq=}}. The remaining 15 shrines were never restored.
The shrine was reconstructed in March 1911.
== Access ==
Ogoso Shrine is surrounded by rice fields and is located slightly off Mie Prefectural Route 717 Iwadetamura Line.
=== Public Transport === * Approximately a 25-minute walk southeast (about 2.0 km) from Tamura Station on the JR Sangu Line.
=== By Car === * Approximately 10 minutes (about 5.0 km) from Tamaki IC on the Ise Expressway. There is no parking available nearby. == References ==

{{draft categories|
[[Category: of the "Enryaku Gishikicho" in 804. The Arakida clan, who developed the area as the sacred rice fields of the Inner Shrine, were based in Ogoso Sone and revered Ogoso Shrine as their clan deity. The "Kenkyu Third Year Annual Events of the Imperial Grand Shrine" (1192) records that the clan visited the shrine on the first Monkey day of the 4th lunar month.
During the Sengoku period, the Arakida clan relocated to Uji, where the Inner Shrine is located, due to the incursion of warrior factions, leading to the decline of Ogoso Shrine. In the early Meiji period, the Grand Shrine of Ise aimed to revive 21 auxiliary and subordinate shrines whose shrines had ceased to exist. In 1874, the restoration of {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyoda Shrine|qq=}}
[[ja:小社神社]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:小社神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17224784]] Wikidata name: Ogoso-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |名称 = 小社神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Ogoso-jinja (Naiku) 01.jpg |所在地 = [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[玉城町]]小社曽根字小社山1955 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 46.8 | 経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 38 | 経度秒 = 52.8 |祭神 = 高水上命 |社格 = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}末社 |創建 = [[延暦]]23rd year （[[804]]）以前 |本殿 = [[神明造]] |別名 = 雨の宮さん |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = [[神嘗祭]] |prefecture= {{ill|Tamaki|WD=Q587348}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.479667|N|136.648|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Ogosō Shrine''' (小社神社) is a [[:en:Setsumatsusha|branch shrine]] of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Inner Shrine) within the [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]] complex. This shrine enshrines a [[:en:Suijin|water deity]], and it is located in an area that was historically the base of the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakida clan|qq=}}, who served as {{ill|negi (Shinto)|ja|禰宜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/negi (Shinto)|1=Negi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594490}} (senior priests) at the Inner Shrine and developed the surrounding region.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing, ed. (2008), p.58.</ref>
== Overview ==
Ogoso Shrine (小社神社) is located in Ogoso Sone, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture. It is one of the 13 auxiliary and subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine (Naiku) of Ise Grand Shrine. Among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine, it Ranks 4th. The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 7,014.9 square meters. Locally, it is affectionately known as "Ame no Miya" or "Ame no Miya-san" and has been a place for rain-making prayers during droughts.
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is Takaminakami no Mikoto (高水上命), a water deity also worshipped at {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakatekunari Shrine|qq=}} within Tamaki Town. The sacred object of worship is a stone.
== History ==
Ogoso Shrine was established by Yamatohime-no-Mikoto. According to the definition of subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, it was founded before the Completion tier 4]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. Ogoso Shrine (小社神社) is located in Ogoso Sone, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture. It is one of the 13 auxiliary and subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine (Naiku) of Ise Grand Shrine. Among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine, it Ranks 4th. The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 7,014.9 square meters. Locally, it is affectionately known as "Ame no Miya" or "Ame no Miya-san" and has been a place for rain-making prayers during droughts.
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is Takaminakami no Mikoto (高水上命), a water deity also worshipped at {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakatekunari Shrine|qq=}} within Tamaki Town. The sacred object of worship is a stone.
== History ==
Ogoso Shrine was established by Yamatohime-no-Mikoto. According to the definition of subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, it was founded before the Completion tier 4]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第3巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第92集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和59th year 3月10日、308p. * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area04/area04_07.jpg 小社神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m08/0804.html 小社神社（おごそじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ 産業観光神社めぐり] - 三重県玉城町 {{125 Shrines of Ise}}
{{translated page|ja|小社神社|version=98019726|comment=Imported full ja history}}
, {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamoshimo Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubura Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} was accomplished. In 1875, a petition was submitted to the Ministry of Religion for the restoration of five more shrines, but it was not granted. However, in 1880, Ogoso Shrine and {{ill|Ashihara Shrine|ja|葭原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihara Shrine|qq=}} were reconstructed using old materials from the East and West storehouses of {{ill|Mishioden Shrine|ja|御塩殿神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishioden Shrine|qq=}}. The remaining 15 shrines were never restored.
The shrine was reconstructed in March 1911.
== Access ==
Ogoso Shrine is surrounded by rice fields and is located slightly off Mie Prefectural Route 717 Iwadetamura Line.
=== Public Transport === * Approximately a 25-minute walk southeast (about 2.0 km) from Tamura Station on the JR Sangu Line.
=== By Car === * Approximately 10 minutes (about 5.0 km) from Tamaki IC on the Ise Expressway. There is no parking available nearby. == References ==

{{draft categories|
[[Category: of the "Enryaku Gishikicho" in 804. The Arakida clan, who developed the area as the sacred rice fields of the Inner Shrine, were based in Ogoso Sone and revered Ogoso Shrine as their clan deity. The "Kenkyu Third Year Annual Events of the Imperial Grand Shrine" (1192) records that the clan visited the shrine on the first Monkey day of the 4th lunar month.
During the Sengoku period, the Arakida clan relocated to Uji, where the Inner Shrine is located, due to the incursion of warrior factions, leading to the decline of Ogoso Shrine. In the early Meiji period, the Grand Shrine of Ise aimed to revive 21 auxiliary and subordinate shrines whose shrines had ceased to exist. In 1874, the restoration of {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyoda Shrine|qq=}}
[[ja:小社神社]]

(2008), p.58.</ref>

This shrine enshrines a [[:en:Suijin|water deity]], and it is located in an area that was historically the base of the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakida clan|qq=}}, who served as {{ill|negi (Shinto)|ja|禰宜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/negi (Shinto)|1=Negi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594490}} (senior priests) at the Inner Shrine and developed the surrounding region.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing, ed. (2008), p.58.</ref>
== Overview ==
Ogoso Shrine (小社神社) is located in Ogoso Sone, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture. It is one of the 13 auxiliary and subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine (Naiku) of Ise Grand Shrine. Among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine, it Ranks 4th. The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 7,014.9 square meters. Locally, it is affectionately known as "Ame no Miya" or "Ame no Miya-san" and has been a place for rain-making prayers during droughts.
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is Takaminakami no Mikoto (高水上命), a water deity also worshipped at {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakatekunari Shrine|qq=}} within Tamaki Town. The sacred object of worship is a stone.
== History ==
Ogoso Shrine was established by Yamatohime-no-Mikoto. According to the definition of subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, it was founded before the Completion tier 4]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第3巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第92集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和59th year 3月10日、308p. * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[下外城田村]]
== External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area04/area04_07.jpg 小社神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m08/0804.html 小社神社（おごそじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ 産業観光神社めぐり] - 三重県玉城町 {{125 Shrines of Ise}}
{{translated page|ja|小社神社|version=98019726|comment=Imported full ja history}}
, {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamoshimo Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubura Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} was accomplished. In 1875, a petition was submitted to the Ministry of Religion for the restoration of five more shrines, but it was not granted. However, in 1880, Ogoso Shrine and {{ill|Ashihara Shrine|ja|葭原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihara Shrine|qq=}} were reconstructed using old materials from the East and West storehouses of {{ill|Mishioden Shrine|ja|御塩殿神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishioden Shrine|qq=}}. The remaining 15 shrines were never restored.
The shrine was reconstructed in March 1911.
== Access ==
Ogoso Shrine is surrounded by rice fields and is located slightly off Mie Prefectural Route 717 Iwadetamura Line.
=== Public Transport === * Approximately a 25-minute walk southeast (about 2.0 km) from Tamura Station on the JR Sangu Line.
=== By Car === * Approximately 10 minutes (about 5.0 km) from Tamaki IC on the Ise Expressway. There is no parking available nearby. == References ==
{{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]]
}}
{{draft categories| <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q17224784}} [[Category: of the "Enryaku Gishikicho" in 804. The Arakida clan, who developed the area as the sacred rice fields of the Inner Shrine, were based in Ogoso Sone and revered Ogoso Shrine as their clan deity. The "Kenkyu Third Year Annual Events of the Imperial Grand Shrine" (1192) records that the clan visited the shrine on the first Monkey day of the 4th lunar month.
During the Sengoku period, the Arakida clan relocated to Uji, where the Inner Shrine is located, due to the incursion of warrior factions, leading to the decline of Ogoso Shrine. In the early Meiji period, the Grand Shrine of Ise aimed to revive 21 auxiliary and subordinate shrines whose shrines had ceased to exist. In 1874, the restoration of {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyoda Shrine|qq=}}
[[ja:小社神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Buildings in Tamaki Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Tamaki]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

The Arakida clan, who developed the area as the sacred rice fields of the Inner Shrine, were based in Ogoso Sone and revered Ogoso Shrine as their clan deity. The "Kenkyu Third Year Annual Events of the Imperial Grand Shrine" (1192) records that the clan visited the shrine on the first Monkey day of the 4th lunar month.
During the Sengoku period, the Arakida clan relocated to Uji, where the Inner Shrine is located, due to the incursion of warrior factions, leading to the decline of Ogoso Shrine. In the early Meiji period, the Grand Shrine of Ise aimed to revive 21 auxiliary and subordinate shrines whose shrines had ceased to exist. In 1874, the restoration of {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyoda Shrine|qq=}}
[[ja:小社神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Buildings in Tamaki Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Tamaki]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:小社神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:小社神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:小社神社]]
{{Infobox religious building |名称 = 小社神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Ogoso-jinja (Naiku) 01.jpg |所在地 = [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[玉城町]]小社曽根字小社山1955 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 46.8 | 経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 38 | 経度秒 = 52.8 |祭神 = 高水上命 |社格 = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}末社 |創建 = [[延暦]]23rd year （[[804]]）以前 |本殿 = [[神明造]] |別名 = 雨の宮さん |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = [[神嘗祭]] |prefecture= {{ill|Tamaki|WD=Q587348}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.479667|N|136.648|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Ogosō Shrine''' (小社神社) is a {{ill|Setsumatsusha|en|Setsumatsusha|WD=Q1071160|lt=branch shrine}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Inner Shrine) within the {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine}} complex. This shrine enshrines a {{ill|Suijin|en|Suijin|WD=Q3137449|lt=water deity}}, and it is located in an area that was historically the base of the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakida clan|qq=}}, who served as {{ill|negi (Shinto)|ja|禰宜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/negi (Shinto)|1=Negi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594490}} (senior priests) at the Inner Shrine and developed the surrounding region.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing, ed. (2008), p.58.</ref>
== Overview ==
Ogoso Shrine (小社神社) is located in Ogoso Sone, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture. It is one of the 13 auxiliary and subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine (Naiku) of Ise Grand Shrine. Among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine, it Ranks 4th. The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 7,014.9 square meters. Locally, it is affectionately known as "Ame no Miya" or "Ame no Miya-san" and has been a place for rain-making prayers during droughts.
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is Takaminakami no Mikoto (高水上命), a water deity also worshipped at {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakatekunari Shrine|qq=}} within Tamaki Town. The sacred object of worship is a stone.
== History ==
Ogoso Shrine was established by Yamatohime-no-Mikoto. According to the definition of subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, it was founded before the Completion tier 4]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第3巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第92集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和59th year 3月10日、308p. * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[下外城田村]]
== External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area04/area04_07.jpg 小社神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m08/0804.html 小社神社（おごそじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ 産業観光神社めぐり] - 三重県玉城町 {{125 Shrines of Ise}}
{{translated page|ja|小社神社|version=98019726|comment=Imported full ja history}}
, {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamoshimo Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubura Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} was accomplished. In 1875, a petition was submitted to the Ministry of Religion for the restoration of five more shrines, but it was not granted. However, in 1880, Ogoso Shrine and {{ill|Ashihara Shrine|ja|葭原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihara Shrine|qq=}} were reconstructed using old materials from the East and West storehouses of {{ill|Mishioden Shrine|ja|御塩殿神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishioden Shrine|qq=}}. The remaining 15 shrines were never restored.
The shrine was reconstructed in March 1911.
== Access ==
Ogoso Shrine is surrounded by rice fields and is located slightly off Mie Prefectural Route 717 Iwadetamura Line.
=== Public Transport === * Approximately a 25-minute walk southeast (about 2.0 km) from Tamura Station on the JR Sangu Line.
=== By Car === * Approximately 10 minutes (about 5.0 km) from Tamaki IC on the Ise Expressway. There is no parking available nearby. == References ==
{{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]]
}}
